Chapter 25

Nephi glories in plainness—Isaiah’s prophecies will be understood in the last days—The Jews will return from Babylon, crucify the Messiah, and be scattered and scourged—They will be restored when they believe in the Messiah—He will first come six hundred years after Lehi left Jerusalem—The Nephites keep the law of Moses and believe in Christ, who is the Holy One of Israel. About 559–545 B.C.

1 Now I, Nephi, do speak somewhat concerning the words which I have written, which have been spoken by the mouth of Isaiah. For behold, Isaiah spake many things which were [ahard](#note1a) for many of my people to understand; for they know not concerning the manner of prophesying among the Jews.

2 For I, Nephi, have not taught them many things concerning the manner of the Jews; for their [aworks](#note2a) were works of darkness, and their doings were doings of abominations.

3 Wherefore, I write unto my people, unto all those that shall receive hereafter these things which I write, [athat](#note3a) they may know the judgments of God, that they come upon all nations, according to the word which he hath spoken.

4 Wherefore, hearken, O my people, which are of the house of Israel, and give ear unto my words; for because the words of Isaiah are not plain unto you, nevertheless they are plain unto all those that are filled with the [aspirit](#note4a) of [bprophecy](#note4b). But I give unto you a [cprophecy](#note4c), according to the spirit which is in me; wherefore I shall prophesy according to the [dplainness](#note4d) which hath been with me from the time that I came out from Jerusalem with my father; for behold, my soul delighteth in [eplainness](#note4e) unto my people, that they may learn.

5 Yea, and my soul delighteth in the words of [aIsaiah](#note5a), for I came out from Jerusalem, and mine eyes hath beheld the things of the [bJews](#note5b), and I know that the Jews do [cunderstand](#note5c) the things of the prophets, and there is none other people that understand the things which were spoken unto the Jews like unto them, save it be that they are taught after the manner of the things of the Jews.

6 But behold, I, Nephi, have not taught my children after the manner of the Jews; but behold, I, of myself, have dwelt at Jerusalem, wherefore I know concerning the regions round about; and I have made mention unto my children concerning the judgments of God, which [ahath](#note6a) come to pass among the Jews, unto my children, according to all that which Isaiah hath spoken, and I do not write them.

7 But behold, I proceed with mine own prophecy, according to my [aplainness](#note7a); in the which I [bknow](#note7b) that no man can err; nevertheless, in the days that the prophecies of Isaiah shall be fulfilled men shall know of a surety, at the times when they shall come to pass.

8 Wherefore, they are of [aworth](#note8a) unto the children of men, and he that supposeth that they are not, unto them will I speak particularly, and confine the words unto mine [bown](#note8b) people; for I know that they shall be of great worth unto them in the [clast days](#note8c); for in that day shall they understand them; wherefore, for their good have I written them.

9 And as one generation hath been [adestroyed](#note9a) among the Jews because of iniquity, even so have they been destroyed from generation to generation according to their iniquities; and never hath any of them been destroyed save it were [bforetold](#note9b) them by the prophets of the Lord.

10 Wherefore, it hath been told them concerning the destruction which should come upon them, immediately after my father left [aJerusalem](#note10a); nevertheless, they [bhardened](#note10b) their hearts; and according to my prophecy they have been destroyed, save it be those which are [ccarried away](#note10c) [dcaptive](#note10d) into Babylon.

11 And now this I speak because of the [aspirit](#note11a) which is in me. And notwithstanding they have been carried away they shall return again, and possess the land of Jerusalem; wherefore, they shall be [brestored](#note11b) again to the [cland](#note11c) of their inheritance.

12 But, behold, they shall have [awars](#note12a), and rumors of wars; and when the day cometh that the [bOnly Begotten](#note12b) of the Father, yea, even the Father of heaven and of earth, shall [cmanifest](#note12c) himself unto them in the flesh, behold, they will reject him, because of their iniquities, and the hardness of their hearts, and the stiffness of their necks.

13 Behold, they will [acrucify](#note13a) him; and after he is laid in a [bsepulchre](#note13b) for the space of [cthree](#note13c) days he shall [drise](#note13d) from the dead, with healing in his wings; and all those who shall believe on his name shall be saved in the kingdom of God. Wherefore, my soul delighteth to prophesy concerning him, for I have [eseen](#note13e) his day, and my heart doth magnify his holy name.

14 And behold it shall come to pass that after the [aMessiah](#note14a) hath risen from the dead, and hath manifested himself unto his people, unto as many as will believe on his name, behold, Jerusalem shall be [bdestroyed](#note14b) again; for [cwo](#note14c) unto them that fight against God and the people of his [dchurch](#note14d).

15 Wherefore, the [aJews](#note15a) shall be [bscattered](#note15b) among all nations; yea, and also [cBabylon](#note15c) shall be destroyed; wherefore, the Jews shall be scattered by other nations.

16 And after they have been [ascattered](#note16a), and the Lord God hath scourged them by other nations for the space of many generations, yea, even down from generation to generation until they shall be persuaded to [bbelieve](#note16b) in Christ, the Son of God, and the atonement, which is infinite for all mankind—and when that day shall come that they shall believe in Christ, and worship the Father in his name, with pure hearts and [cclean hands](#note16c), and look not forward any more for [danother](#note16d) Messiah, then, at that time, the day will come that it must needs be expedient that they should believe these things.

17 And the Lord will set his hand again the second time to [arestore](#note17a) his people from their lost and fallen state. Wherefore, he will proceed to do a [bmarvelous work](#note17b) and a wonder among the children of men.

18 Wherefore, he shall bring forth [ahis](#note18a) [bwords](#note18b) unto them, which words shall [cjudge](#note18c) them at the last day, for they shall be given them for the purpose of [dconvincing](#note18d) them of the true Messiah, who was rejected by them; and unto the convincing of them that they need not look forward any more for a Messiah to come, for there should not any come, save it should be a [efalse Messiah](#note18e) which should deceive the people; for there is save one [fMessiah](#note18f) spoken of by the prophets, and that Messiah is he who should be rejected of the Jews.

19 For according to the words of the prophets, the [aMessiah](#note19a) cometh in [bsix hundred](#note19b) years from the time that my father left Jerusalem; and according to the words of the prophets, and also the word of the [cangel](#note19c) of God, his [dname](#note19d) shall be Jesus Christ, the [eSon of God](#note19e).

20 And now, my brethren, I have spoken plainly that ye cannot err. And as the Lord God liveth that [abrought](#note20a) Israel up out of the land of Egypt, and gave unto Moses power that he should [bheal](#note20b) the nations after they had been bitten by the poisonous serpents, if they would cast their eyes unto the [cserpent](#note20c) which he did raise up before them, and also gave him power that he should smite the [drock](#note20d) and the water should come forth; yea, behold I say unto you, that as these things are [etrue](#note20e), and as the Lord God liveth, there is none other [fname](#note20f) given under heaven save it be this Jesus Christ, of which I have spoken, whereby man can be saved.

21 Wherefore, for this cause hath the Lord God promised unto me that these things which I [awrite](#note21a) shall be kept and preserved, and handed down unto my seed, from generation to generation, that the promise may be fulfilled unto Joseph, that his seed should never [bperish](#note21b) as long as the earth should stand.

22 Wherefore, these things shall go from generation to generation as long as the earth shall stand; and they shall go according to the will and pleasure of God; and the nations who shall possess them shall be [ajudged](#note22a) of them according to the words which are written.

23 For we labor diligently to write, to [apersuade](#note23a) our children, and also our brethren, to believe in Christ, and to be reconciled to God; for we know that it is by [bgrace](#note23b) that we are saved, after all we can [cdo](#note23c).

24 And, notwithstanding we believe in Christ, we [akeep](#note24a) the law of Moses, and look forward with steadfastness unto Christ, until the law shall be fulfilled.

25 For, for this end was the [alaw](#note25a) given; wherefore the law hath become [bdead](#note25b) unto us, and we are made alive in Christ because of our faith; yet we keep the law because of the commandments.

26 And we [atalk](#note26a) of Christ, we rejoice in Christ, we preach of Christ, we [bprophesy](#note26b) of Christ, and we write according to our prophecies, that our [cchildren](#note26c) may know to what source they may look for a [dremission](#note26d) of their sins.

27 Wherefore, we speak concerning the law that our children may know the deadness of the law; and they, by knowing the deadness of the law, may look forward unto that life which is in Christ, and know for what end the law was given. And after the law is fulfilled in Christ, that they need not harden their hearts against him when the law ought to be done away.

28 And now behold, my people, ye are a [astiffnecked](#note28a) people; wherefore, I have spoken plainly unto you, that ye cannot misunderstand. And the words which I have spoken shall stand as a [btestimony](#note28b) against you; for they are sufficient to [cteach](#note28c) any man the [dright](#note28d) way; for the right way is to believe in Christ and deny him not; for by denying him ye also deny the prophets and the law.

29 And now behold, I say unto you that the right way is to believe in Christ, and deny him not; and Christ is the Holy One of Israel; wherefore ye must bow down before him, and [aworship](#note29a) him with all your [bmight](#note29b), mind, and strength, and your whole soul; and if ye do this ye shall in nowise be cast out.

30 And, inasmuch as it shall be expedient, ye must keep the [aperformances](#note30a) and [bordinances](#note30b) of God until the law shall be fulfilled which was given unto Moses.

  [Jacob 4:14](../jacob/4.xhtml#p14). TG Symbolism.

  TG Ordinance.

Chapter 26

Christ will minister to the Nephites—Nephi foresees the destruction of his people—They will speak from the dust—The Gentiles will build up false churches and secret combinations—The Lord forbids men to practice priestcrafts. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And after Christ shall have [arisen](#note1a) from the dead he shall [bshow](#note1b) himself unto you, my children, and my beloved brethren; and the words which he shall speak unto you shall be the [claw](#note1c) which ye shall do.

2 For behold, I say unto you that I have beheld that many generations shall pass away, and there shall be great wars and contentions among my people.

3 And after the Messiah shall come there shall be [asigns](#note3a) given unto my people of his [bbirth](#note3b), and also of his [cdeath](#note3c) and resurrection; and great and terrible shall that day be unto the wicked, for they shall perish; and they perish because they cast out the [dprophets](#note3d), and the saints, and stone them, and slay them; wherefore the cry of the [eblood](#note3e) of the saints shall ascend up to God from the ground against them.

4 Wherefore, all those who are proud, and that do wickedly, the day that cometh shall [aburn](#note4a) them up, saith the Lord of Hosts, for they shall be as stubble.

5 And they that kill the [aprophets](#note5a), and the saints, the depths of the earth shall [bswallow](#note5b) them up, saith the Lord of Hosts; and [cmountains](#note5c) shall cover them, and whirlwinds shall carry them away, and buildings shall fall upon them and crush them to pieces and grind them to powder.

6 And they shall be visited with thunderings, and lightnings, and earthquakes, and all manner of destructions, for the [afire](#note6a) of the anger of the Lord shall be kindled against them, and they shall be as stubble, and the day that cometh shall consume them, saith the Lord of Hosts.

7 [aO](#note7a) the pain, and the anguish of my soul for the loss of the slain of my people! For I, Nephi, have seen it, and it well nigh consumeth me before the presence of the Lord; but I must cry unto my God: Thy ways are [bjust](#note7b).

8 But behold, the righteous that hearken unto the words of the prophets, and destroy them not, but look forward unto Christ with [asteadfastness](#note8a) for the signs which are given, notwithstanding all [bpersecution](#note8b)—behold, they are they which shall [cnot perish](#note8c).

9 But the Son of Righteousness shall [aappear](#note9a) unto them; and he shall [bheal](#note9b) them, and they shall have [cpeace](#note9c) with him, until [dthree](#note9d) generations shall have passed away, and many of the [efourth](#note9e) generation shall have passed away in righteousness.

10 And when these things have passed away a speedy [adestruction](#note10a) cometh unto my people; for, notwithstanding the pains of my soul, I have seen it; wherefore, I know that it shall come to pass; and they sell themselves for naught; for, for the reward of their pride and their [bfoolishness](#note10b) they shall reap destruction; for because they yield unto the devil and [cchoose](#note10c) works of [ddarkness](#note10d) rather than light, therefore they must go down to [ehell](#note10e).

11 For the Spirit of the Lord will not always [astrive](#note11a) with man. And when the Spirit [bceaseth](#note11b) to strive with man then cometh speedy destruction, and this grieveth my soul.

12 And as I spake concerning the [aconvincing](#note12a) of the [bJews](#note12b), that Jesus is the [cvery Christ](#note12c), it must needs be that the Gentiles be convinced also that Jesus is the Christ, the [dEternal](#note12d) [eGod](#note12e);

13 And that he [amanifesteth](#note13a) himself unto all those who believe in him, by the power of the [bHoly Ghost](#note13b); yea, unto every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, working mighty [cmiracles](#note13c), signs, and wonders, among the children of men according to their [dfaith](#note13d).

14 But behold, I prophesy unto you concerning the [alast days](#note14a); concerning the days when the Lord God shall [bbring](#note14b) these things forth unto the children of men.

15 After my seed and the seed of my brethren shall have [adwindled](#note15a) in unbelief, and shall have been smitten by the Gentiles; yea, after the Lord God shall have [bcamped](#note15b) against them round about, and shall have laid siege against them with a mount, and raised forts against them; and after they shall have been brought down low in the dust, even that they are not, yet the words of the righteous shall be written, and the [cprayers](#note15c) of the faithful shall be heard, and all those who have [ddwindled](#note15d) in unbelief shall not be forgotten.

16 For those who shall be destroyed shall [aspeak](#note16a) unto them out of the ground, and their speech shall be low out of the dust, and their voice shall be as one that hath a familiar spirit; for the Lord God will give unto him power, that he may whisper concerning them, even as it were out of the ground; and their speech shall whisper out of the dust.

17 For thus saith the Lord God: They shall [awrite](#note17a) the things which shall be done among them, and they shall be written and [bsealed](#note17b) up in a book, and those who have dwindled in [cunbelief](#note17c) shall not have them, for they [dseek](#note17d) to destroy the things of God.

18 Wherefore, as those who have been destroyed have been destroyed speedily; and the multitude of their [aterrible ones](#note18a) shall be as [bchaff](#note18b) that passeth away—yea, thus saith the Lord God: It shall be at an instant, suddenly—

19 And it shall come to pass, that those who have dwindled in unbelief shall be [asmitten](#note19a) by the hand of the Gentiles.

20 And the Gentiles are lifted up in the [apride](#note20a) of their eyes, and have [bstumbled](#note20b), because of the greatness of their [cstumbling block](#note20c), that they have built up many [dchurches](#note20d); nevertheless, they [eput down](#note20e) the power and miracles of God, and preach up unto themselves their own wisdom and their own [flearning](#note20f), that they may get gain and [ggrind](#note20g) upon the face of the poor.

21 And there are many churches built up which cause [aenvyings](#note21a), and [bstrifes](#note21b), and [cmalice](#note21c).

22 And there are also secret [acombinations](#note22a), even as in times of old, according to the combinations of the [bdevil](#note22b), for he is the founder of all these things; yea, the founder of murder, and [cworks](#note22c) of darkness; yea, and he leadeth them by the neck with a flaxen cord, until he bindeth them with his strong cords forever.

23 For behold, my beloved brethren, I say unto you that the Lord God worketh not in [adarkness](#note23a).

24 He doeth not [aanything](#note24a) save it be for the benefit of the world; for he [bloveth](#note24b) the world, even that he layeth down his own life that he may draw [call](#note24c) men unto him. Wherefore, he commandeth none that they shall not partake of his salvation.

25 Behold, doth he cry unto any, saying: Depart from me? Behold, I say unto you, Nay; but he saith: [aCome](#note25a) unto me all ye [bends](#note25b) of the earth, [cbuy](#note25c) milk and honey, without money and without price.

26 Behold, hath he commanded any that they should [adepart](#note26a) out of the synagogues, or out of the houses of worship? Behold, I say unto you, Nay.

27 Hath he commanded any that they should not partake of his [asalvation](#note27a)? Behold I say unto you, Nay; but he hath [bgiven](#note27b) it free for all men; and he hath commanded his people that they should persuade all men to [crepentance](#note27c).

28 Behold, hath the Lord commanded any that they should not partake of his goodness? Behold I say unto you, Nay; but [aall](#note28a) men are privileged the one [blike](#note28b) unto the other, and none are forbidden.

29 He commandeth that there shall be no [apriestcrafts](#note29a); for, behold, priestcrafts are that men preach and set [bthemselves](#note29b) up for a light unto the world, that they may get [cgain](#note29c) and [dpraise](#note29d) of the world; but they seek not the [ewelfare](#note29e) of Zion.

30 Behold, the Lord hath forbidden this thing; wherefore, the Lord God hath given a commandment that all men should have [acharity](#note30a), which [bcharity](#note30b) is [clove](#note30c). And except they should have charity they were nothing. Wherefore, if they should have charity they would not suffer the laborer in Zion to perish.

31 But the [alaborer](#note31a) in [bZion](#note31b) shall labor for Zion; for if they labor for [cmoney](#note31c) they shall perish.

32 And again, the Lord God hath [acommanded](#note32a) that men should not murder; that they should not lie; that they should not [bsteal](#note32b); that they should not take the name of the Lord their God in [cvain](#note32c); that they should not [denvy](#note32d); that they should not have [emalice](#note32e); that they should not contend one with another; that they should not commit [fwhoredoms](#note32f); and that they should do none of these things; for whoso doeth them shall perish.

33 For none of these iniquities come of the Lord; for he doeth that which is good among the children of men; and he doeth nothing save it be plain unto the children of men; and he [ainviteth](#note33a) them [ball](#note33b) to [ccome](#note33c) unto him and partake of his goodness; and he [ddenieth](#note33d) none that come unto him, black and white, [ebond](#note33e) and free, male and female; and he remembereth the [fheathen](#note33f); and all are alike unto God, both Jew and Gentile.

Chapter 27

Darkness and apostasy will cover the earth in the last days—The Book of Mormon will come forth—Three witnesses will testify of the book—The learned man will say he cannot read the sealed book—The Lord will do a marvelous work and a wonder—Compare Isaiah 29. About 559–545 B.C.

1 But, behold, in the [alast days](#note1a), or in the days of the Gentiles—yea, behold all the nations of the Gentiles and also the Jews, both those who shall come upon this land and those who shall be upon other lands, yea, even upon all the lands of the earth, behold, they will be [bdrunken](#note1b) with iniquity and all manner of abominations—

2 And when that day shall come they shall be [avisited](#note2a) of the Lord of Hosts, with thunder and with earthquake, and with a great noise, and with storm, and with tempest, and with the [bflame](#note2b) of devouring fire.

3 And all the [anations](#note3a) that [bfight](#note3b) against Zion, and that distress her, shall be as a dream of a night vision; yea, it shall be unto them, even as unto a hungry man which dreameth, and behold he eateth but he awaketh and his soul is empty; or like unto a thirsty man which dreameth, and behold he drinketh but he awaketh and behold he is faint, and his soul hath appetite; yea, even so shall the multitude of all the nations be that fight against Mount Zion.

4 For behold, all ye that doeth iniquity, stay yourselves and wonder, for ye shall cry out, and cry; yea, ye shall be [adrunken](#note4a) but not with wine, ye shall stagger but not with strong drink.

5 For behold, the Lord hath poured out upon you the spirit of deep sleep. For behold, ye have closed your [aeyes](#note5a), and ye have [brejected](#note5b) the prophets; and your rulers, and the seers hath he covered because of your iniquity.

6 And it shall come to pass that the Lord God shall bring forth unto [ayou](#note6a) the words of a [bbook](#note6b), and they shall be the words of them which have slumbered.

7 And behold the book shall be [asealed](#note7a); and in the book shall be a [brevelation](#note7b) from God, from the beginning of the world to the [cending](#note7c) thereof.

8 Wherefore, because of the things which are [asealed](#note8a) up, the things which are sealed shall not be delivered in the day of the wickedness and abominations of the people. Wherefore the book shall be kept from them.

9 But the book shall be delivered unto a man, and he shall deliver the words of the book, which are the words of those who have slumbered in the dust, and he shall deliver these words unto [aanother](#note9a);

10 But the words which are [asealed](#note10a) he shall not deliver, neither shall he deliver the book. For the book shall be sealed by the power of God, and the revelation which was sealed shall be kept in the book until the own due time of the Lord, that they may come forth; for behold, they [breveal](#note10b) all things from the foundation of the world unto the end thereof.

11 And the day cometh that the words of the book which were sealed shall be read upon the house tops; and they shall be read by the power of Christ; and all things shall be [arevealed](#note11a) unto the children of men which ever have been among the children of men, and which ever will be even unto the end of the earth.

12 Wherefore, at that day when the book shall be delivered unto the man of whom I have spoken, the book shall be hid from the eyes of the world, that the eyes of none shall behold it save it be that [athree](#note12a) [bwitnesses](#note12b) shall behold it, by the power of God, besides him to whom the book shall be delivered; and they shall testify to the truth of the book and the things therein.

13 And there is [anone](#note13a) other which shall view it, save it be a few according to the will of God, to bear testimony of his word unto the children of men; for the Lord God hath said that the words of the faithful should speak as if it were [bfrom the dead](#note13b).

14 Wherefore, the Lord God will proceed to bring forth the words of the book; and in the mouth of as many witnesses as seemeth him good will he establish his word; and wo be unto him that [arejecteth](#note14a) the word of God!

15 But behold, it shall come to pass that the Lord God shall say unto him to whom he shall deliver the book: Take these words which are not sealed and deliver them to another, that he may show them unto the learned, saying: [aRead](#note15a) this, I pray thee. And the learned shall say: Bring hither the book, and I will read them.

16 And now, because of the glory of the world and to get [again](#note16a) will they say this, and not for the glory of God.

17 And the man shall say: I cannot bring the book, for it is sealed.

18 Then shall the learned say: I cannot read it.

19 Wherefore it shall come to pass, that the Lord God will [adeliver](#note19a) again the book and the words thereof to him that is not learned; and the man that is not learned shall say: I am not learned.

20 Then shall the Lord God say unto him: The learned shall not read them, for they have rejected them, and I am [aable](#note20a) to do mine own work; wherefore thou shalt read the words which I shall give unto thee.

21 [aTouch not](#note21a) the things which are sealed, for I will bring them forth in mine own due time; for I will show unto the children of men that I am able to do mine own work.

22 Wherefore, when thou hast read the words which I have commanded thee, and obtained the [awitnesses](#note22a) which I have promised unto thee, then shalt thou seal up the book again, and hide it up unto me, that I may preserve the words which thou hast not read, until I shall see fit in mine own [bwisdom](#note22b) to [creveal](#note22c) all things unto the children of men.

23 For behold, I am God; and I am a God of [amiracles](#note23a); and I will show unto the [bworld](#note23b) that I am the same yesterday, today, and forever; and I [cwork](#note23c) not among the children of men save it be [daccording](#note23d) to their faith.

24 And again it shall come to pass that the Lord shall say unto him that shall read the words that shall be delivered him:

25 [aForasmuch](#note25a) as this people draw near unto me with their mouth, and with their lips do [bhonor](#note25b) me, but have removed their [chearts](#note25c) far from me, and their fear towards me is taught by the [dprecepts](#note25d) of men—

26 Therefore, I will proceed to do a [amarvelous work](#note26a) among this people, yea, a [bmarvelous work](#note26b) and a wonder, for the [cwisdom](#note26c) of their wise and [dlearned](#note26d) shall perish, and the [eunderstanding](#note26e) of their [fprudent](#note26f) shall be hid.

27 And [awo](#note27a) unto them that seek deep to hide their [bcounsel](#note27b) from the Lord! And their works are in the [cdark](#note27c); and they say: Who seeth us, and who knoweth us? And they also say: Surely, your turning of things upside down shall be esteemed as the [dpotter’s](#note27d) clay. But behold, I will show unto them, saith the Lord of Hosts, that I [eknow](#note27e) all their works. For shall the work say of him that made it, he made me not? Or shall the thing framed say of him that framed it, he had no understanding?

28 But behold, saith the Lord of Hosts: I will show unto the children of men that it is yet a very little while and Lebanon shall be turned into a fruitful field; and the [afruitful](#note28a) field shall be esteemed as a forest.

29 [aAnd](#note29a) in that day shall the [bdeaf](#note29b) hear the words of the book, and the eyes of the blind shall see out of obscurity and out of darkness.

30 And the [ameek](#note30a) also shall increase, and their [bjoy](#note30b) shall be in the Lord, and the poor among men shall rejoice in the Holy One of Israel.

31 For assuredly as the Lord liveth they shall see that the [aterrible one](#note31a) is brought to naught, and the scorner is consumed, and all that watch for iniquity are cut off;

32 And they that make a man an [aoffender](#note32a) for a word, and lay a snare for him that reproveth in the [bgate](#note32b), and [cturn](#note32c) aside the just for a thing of naught.

33 Therefore, thus saith the Lord, who redeemed Abraham, concerning the house of Jacob: Jacob shall [anot now](#note33a) be ashamed, neither shall his face now wax pale.

34 But when he [aseeth](#note34a) his children, the work of my hands, in the midst of him, they shall sanctify my name, and sanctify the Holy One of Jacob, and shall fear the God of Israel.

35 They also that [aerred](#note35a) in spirit shall come to understanding, and they that murmured shall [blearn](#note35b) doctrine.

Chapter 28

Many false churches will be built up in the last days—They will teach false, vain, and foolish doctrines—Apostasy will abound because of false teachers—The devil will rage in the hearts of men—He will teach all manner of false doctrines. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And now, behold, my brethren, I have spoken unto you, according as the Spirit hath constrained me; wherefore, I know that they must surely come to pass.

2 And the things which shall be written out of the [abook](#note2a) shall be of great [bworth](#note2b) unto the children of men, and especially unto our seed, which is a [cremnant](#note2c) of the house of Israel.

3 For it shall come to pass in that day that the [achurches](#note3a) which are built up, and not unto the Lord, when the one shall say unto the other: Behold, I, I am the Lord’s; and the others shall say: I, I am the Lord’s; and thus shall every one say that hath built up [bchurches](#note3b), and not unto the Lord—

4 And they shall contend one with another; and their priests shall contend one with another, and they shall teach with their [alearning](#note4a), and deny the [bHoly Ghost](#note4b), which giveth utterance.

5 And they [adeny](#note5a) the [bpower](#note5b) of God, the Holy One of Israel; and they say unto the people: Hearken unto us, and hear ye our precept; for behold there is [cno](#note5c) God today, for the Lord and the Redeemer hath done his work, and he hath given his power unto men;

6 Behold, hearken ye unto my precept; if they shall say there is a miracle wrought by the hand of the Lord, believe it not; for this day he is not a God of [amiracles](#note6a); he hath done his work.

7 Yea, and there shall be many which shall say: [aEat](#note7a), drink, and be merry, for tomorrow we die; and it shall be well with us.

8 And there shall also be many which shall say: [aEat](#note8a), drink, and be [bmerry](#note8b); nevertheless, fear God—he will [cjustify](#note8c) in committing a little [dsin](#note8d); yea, [elie](#note8e) a little, take the advantage of one because of his words, dig a [fpit](#note8f) for thy neighbor; there is [gno](#note8g) harm in this; and do all these things, for tomorrow we die; and if it so be that we are guilty, God will beat us with a few stripes, and at last we shall be saved in the kingdom of God.

9 Yea, and there shall be many which shall teach after this manner, [afalse](#note9a) and vain and [bfoolish](#note9b) [cdoctrines](#note9c), and shall be puffed up in their hearts, and shall seek deep to hide their counsels from the Lord; and their works shall be in the dark.

10 And the [ablood](#note10a) of the saints shall cry from the ground against them.

11 Yea, they have all gone out of the [away](#note11a); they have become [bcorrupted](#note11b).

12 Because of [apride](#note12a), and because of [bfalse](#note12b) teachers, and [cfalse doctrine](#note12c), their churches have become corrupted, and their churches are lifted up; because of pride they are puffed up.

13 They [arob](#note13a) the [bpoor](#note13b) because of their fine sanctuaries; they rob the poor because of their fine clothing; and they persecute the meek and the poor in heart, because in their [cpride](#note13c) they are puffed up.

14 They wear [astiff](#note14a) necks and high heads; yea, and because of pride, and wickedness, and abominations, and [bwhoredoms](#note14b), they have all [cgone astray](#note14c) save it be a [dfew](#note14d), who are the humble followers of Christ; nevertheless, they are [eled](#note14e), that in many instances they do [ferr](#note14f) because they are taught by the precepts of men.

15 O the [awise](#note15a), and the learned, and the rich, that are puffed up in the [bpride](#note15b) of their [chearts](#note15c), and all those who preach [dfalse doctrines](#note15d), and all those who commit [ewhoredoms](#note15e), and pervert the right way of the Lord, [fwo](#note15f), wo, wo be unto them, saith the Lord God Almighty, for they shall be thrust down to hell!

16 Wo unto them that [aturn](#note16a) aside the just for a thing of naught and [brevile](#note16b) against that which is good, and say that it is of no worth! For the day shall come that the Lord God will speedily visit the inhabitants of the earth; and in that day that they are [cfully](#note16c) ripe in iniquity they shall perish.

17 But behold, if the inhabitants of the earth shall repent of their wickedness and abominations they shall not be destroyed, saith the Lord of Hosts.

18 But behold, that great and [aabominable](#note18a) church, the [bwhore](#note18b) of all the earth, must [ctumble](#note18c) to the earth, and great must be the fall thereof.

19 For the kingdom of the devil must [ashake](#note19a), and they which belong to it must needs be stirred up unto repentance, or the [bdevil](#note19b) will grasp them with his everlasting [cchains](#note19c), and they be stirred up to anger, and perish;

20 For behold, at that day shall he [arage](#note20a) in the [bhearts](#note20b) of the children of men, and stir them up to anger against that which is good.

21 And others will he [apacify](#note21a), and lull them away into carnal [bsecurity](#note21b), that they will say: All is well in Zion; yea, Zion prospereth, all is well—and thus the [cdevil](#note21c) [dcheateth](#note21d) their souls, and leadeth them away carefully down to hell.

22 And behold, others he [aflattereth](#note22a) away, and telleth them there is no [bhell](#note22b); and he saith unto them: I am no devil, for there is none—and thus he whispereth in their ears, until he grasps them with his awful [cchains](#note22c), from whence there is no deliverance.

23 Yea, they are grasped with death, and hell; and death, and hell, and the devil, and all that have been seized therewith must stand before the throne of God, and be [ajudged](#note23a) according to their works, from whence they must go into the place prepared for them, even a [blake](#note23b) of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment.

24 Therefore, wo be unto him that is at [aease](#note24a) in Zion!

25 Wo be unto him that crieth: All is well!

26 Yea, wo be unto him that [ahearkeneth](#note26a) unto the precepts of men, and denieth the power of God, and the gift of the Holy Ghost!

27 Yea, wo be unto him that saith: We have received, and we [aneed](#note27a) no more!

28 And in fine, wo unto all those who tremble, and are [aangry](#note28a) because of [bthe truth](#note28b) of God! For behold, he that is built upon the [crock](#note28c) [dreceiveth](#note28d) it with gladness; and he that is built upon a sandy foundation trembleth lest he shall fall.

29 Wo be unto him that shall say: We have received the word of God, and we [aneed](#note29a) [bno more](#note29b) of the word of God, for we have enough!

30 For behold, thus saith the Lord God: I will give unto the children of men line upon line, [aprecept](#note30a) upon precept, here a little and there a little; and blessed are those who hearken unto my precepts, and lend an ear unto my counsel, for they shall learn [bwisdom](#note30b); for unto him that [creceiveth](#note30c) I will give [dmore](#note30d); and from them that shall say, We have enough, from them shall be taken away even that which they have.

31 Cursed is he that putteth his [atrust](#note31a) in man, or maketh flesh his arm, or shall hearken unto the [bprecepts](#note31b) of men, save their precepts shall be given by the power of the Holy Ghost.

32 [aWo](#note32a) be unto the Gentiles, saith the Lord God of Hosts! For notwithstanding I shall lengthen out mine arm unto them from day to day, they will deny me; nevertheless, I will be merciful unto them, saith the Lord God, if they will repent and [bcome](#note32b) unto me; for mine [carm](#note32c) is lengthened out all the day long, saith the Lord God of Hosts.

Chapter 29

Many Gentiles will reject the Book of Mormon—They will say, We need no more Bible—The Lord speaks to many nations—He will judge the world out of the books which will be written. About 559–545 B.C.

1 But behold, there shall be many—at that day when I shall proceed to do a [amarvelous work](#note1a) among them, that I may remember my [bcovenants](#note1b) which I have made unto the children of men, that I may set my hand again the [csecond](#note1c) time to recover my people, which are of the house of Israel;

2 And also, that I may remember the promises which I have made unto thee, Nephi, and also unto thy father, that I would remember your seed; and that the [awords](#note2a) of your seed should proceed forth out of my mouth unto your seed; and my words shall [bhiss](#note2b) forth unto the [cends](#note2c) of the earth, for a [dstandard](#note2d) unto my people, which are of the house of Israel;

3 And because my words shall hiss forth—many of the Gentiles shall say: A [aBible](#note3a)! A Bible! We have got a Bible, and there cannot be any more Bible.

4 But thus saith the Lord God: O fools, they shall have a [aBible](#note4a); and it shall proceed forth from the [bJews](#note4b), mine ancient covenant people. And what [cthank](#note4c) they the [dJews](#note4d) for the Bible which they receive from them? Yea, what do the Gentiles mean? Do they remember the travails, and the labors, and the pains of the Jews, and their diligence unto me, in bringing forth salvation unto the Gentiles?

5 O ye Gentiles, have ye remembered the Jews, mine ancient covenant people? Nay; but ye have [acursed](#note5a) them, and have [bhated](#note5b) them, and have not sought to recover them. But behold, I will return all these things upon your own heads; for I the Lord have not forgotten my people.

6 Thou fool, that shall say: A [aBible](#note6a), we have got a Bible, and we need no more Bible. Have ye obtained a Bible save it were by the Jews?

7 Know ye not that there are more [anations](#note7a) than one? Know ye not that I, the Lord your God, have created all men, and that I remember those who are upon the [bisles](#note7b) of the sea; and that I rule in the heavens above and in the [cearth](#note7c) beneath; and I bring forth my [dword](#note7d) unto the children of men, yea, even upon all the nations of the earth?

8 Wherefore murmur ye, because that ye shall receive more of my word? Know ye not that the [atestimony](#note8a) of [btwo](#note8b) nations is a [cwitness](#note8c) unto you that I am God, that I remember one [dnation](#note8d) like unto another? Wherefore, I speak the same words unto one nation like unto another. And when the two [enations](#note8e) shall run together the testimony of the two nations shall run together also.

9 And I do this that I may prove unto many that I am the [asame](#note9a) yesterday, today, and forever; and that I speak forth my [bwords](#note9b) according to mine own pleasure. And because that I have spoken one [cword](#note9c) ye need not suppose that I cannot speak another; for my [dwork](#note9d) is not yet finished; neither shall it be until the end of man, neither from that time henceforth and forever.

10 Wherefore, because that ye have a Bible ye need not suppose that it contains all my [awords](#note10a); neither [bneed](#note10b) ye suppose that I have not caused more to be written.

11 For I command [aall](#note11a) men, both in the east and in the west, and in the north, and in the south, and in the islands of the sea, that they shall [bwrite](#note11b) the words which I speak unto them; for out of the [cbooks](#note11c) which shall be written I will [djudge](#note11d) the world, every man according to their works, according to that which is written.

12 For behold, I shall speak unto the [aJews](#note12a) and they shall [bwrite](#note12b) it; and I shall also speak unto the Nephites and they shall [cwrite](#note12c) it; and I shall also speak unto the other tribes of the house of Israel, which I have led away, and they shall write it; and I shall also speak unto [dall](#note12d) nations of the earth and they shall write it.

13 And it shall come to pass that the [aJews](#note13a) shall have the words of the Nephites, and the Nephites shall have the words of the Jews; and the Nephites and the Jews shall have the words of the [blost tribes](#note13b) of Israel; and the lost tribes of Israel shall have the words of the Nephites and the Jews.

14 And it shall come to pass that my people, which are of the [ahouse of Israel](#note14a), shall be gathered home unto the [blands](#note14b) of their possessions; and my word also shall be gathered in [cone](#note14c). And I will show unto them that fight against my word and against my [dpeople](#note14d), who are of the [ehouse of Israel](#note14e), that I am God, and that I [fcovenanted](#note14f) with [gAbraham](#note14g) that I would remember his [hseed](#note14h) [iforever](#note14i).

Chapter 30

Converted Gentiles will be numbered with the covenant people—Many Lamanites and Jews will believe the word and become delightsome—Israel will be restored and the wicked destroyed. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And now behold, my beloved brethren, I would speak unto you; for I, Nephi, would not suffer that ye should suppose that ye are more righteous than the Gentiles shall be. For behold, except ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall all likewise [aperish](#note1a); and because of the words which have been spoken ye need not suppose that the Gentiles are utterly destroyed.

2 For behold, I say unto you that as many of the Gentiles as will repent are the [acovenant](#note2a) people of the Lord; and as many of the [bJews](#note2b) as will not repent shall be [ccast off](#note2c); for the Lord [dcovenanteth](#note2d) with none save it be with them that [erepent](#note2e) and believe in his Son, who is the Holy One of Israel.

3 And now, I would prophesy somewhat more concerning the Jews and the Gentiles. For after the book of which I have spoken shall come forth, and be written unto the Gentiles, and sealed up again unto the Lord, there shall be many which shall [abelieve](#note3a) the words which are written; and [bthey](#note3b) shall carry them forth unto the [cremnant](#note3c) of our seed.

4 And then shall the remnant of our seed know concerning us, how that we came out from Jerusalem, and that they are descendants of the Jews.

5 And the gospel of Jesus Christ shall be declared among [athem](#note5a); wherefore, [bthey](#note5b) shall be restored unto the [cknowledge](#note5c) of their fathers, and also to the knowledge of Jesus Christ, which was had among their fathers.

6 And then shall they rejoice; for they shall [aknow](#note6a) that it is a blessing unto them from the hand of God; and their [bscales](#note6b) of darkness shall begin to fall from their eyes; and many generations shall not pass away among them, save they shall be a pure and a [cdelightsome](#note6c) people.

7 And it shall come to pass that the [aJews](#note7a) which are scattered also shall [bbegin](#note7b) to believe in Christ; and they shall begin to gather in upon the face of the land; and as many as shall believe in Christ shall also become a delightsome people.

8 And it shall come to pass that the Lord God shall commence his work among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people, to bring about the [arestoration](#note8a) of his people upon the earth.

9 And with righteousness shall the [aLord God](#note9a) [bjudge](#note9b) the poor, and reprove with equity for the [cmeek](#note9c) of the earth. And he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth; and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.

10 For the [atime](#note10a) speedily cometh that the Lord God shall cause a great [bdivision](#note10b) among the people, and the wicked will he [cdestroy](#note10c); and he will [dspare](#note10d) his people, yea, even if it so be that he must [edestroy](#note10e) the wicked by fire.

11 And [arighteousness](#note11a) shall be the girdle of his loins, and faithfulness the girdle of his reins.

12 [aAnd](#note12a) then shall the wolf dwell with the lamb; and the leopard shall lie down with the kid, and the calf, and the young lion, and the fatling, together; and a little child shall lead them.

13 And the cow and the bear shall feed; their young ones shall lie down together; and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.

14 And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp, and the weaned child shall put his hand on the cockatrice’s den.

15 They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy [amountain](#note15a); for the earth shall be full of the [bknowledge](#note15b) of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.

16 Wherefore, the things of [aall](#note16a) nations shall be made known; yea, all things shall be made [bknown](#note16b) unto the children of men.

17 There is nothing which is secret save it shall be [arevealed](#note17a); there is no work of darkness save it shall be made manifest in the light; and there is nothing which is sealed upon the earth save it shall be loosed.

18 Wherefore, all things which have been revealed unto the children of men shall at that [aday](#note18a) be revealed; and Satan shall have power over the hearts of the children of men [bno more](#note18b), for a long time. And now, my beloved brethren, I make an end of my sayings.

Chapter 31

Nephi tells why Christ was baptized—Men must follow Christ, be baptized, receive the Holy Ghost, and endure to the end to be saved—Repentance and baptism are the gate to the strait and narrow path—Eternal life comes to those who keep the commandments after baptism. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And now I, Nephi, make an end of my [aprophesying](#note1a) unto you, my beloved brethren. And I cannot write but a few things, which I know must surely come to pass; neither can I write but a few of the [bwords](#note1b) of my brother Jacob.

2 Wherefore, the things which I have written sufficeth me, save it be a few words which I [amust](#note2a) speak concerning the doctrine of Christ; wherefore, I shall speak unto you plainly, according to the plainness of my prophesying.

3 For my soul delighteth in [aplainness](#note3a); for after this manner doth the Lord God work among the children of men. For the Lord God giveth light unto the [bunderstanding](#note3b); for he speaketh unto men according to their [clanguage](#note3c), unto their understanding.

4 Wherefore, I would that ye should remember that I have spoken unto you concerning that [aprophet](#note4a) which the Lord showed unto me, that should baptize the [bLamb](#note4b) of God, which should take away the sins of the world.

5 And now, if the Lamb of God, he being [aholy](#note5a), should have need to be [bbaptized](#note5b) by water, to fulfil all righteousness, O then, how much more need have we, being unholy, to be [cbaptized](#note5c), yea, even by water!

6 And now, I would ask of you, my beloved brethren, wherein the Lamb of God did fulfil all righteousness in being baptized by water?

7 Know ye not that he was holy? But notwithstanding he being holy, he showeth unto the children of men that, according to the flesh he humbleth himself before the Father, and witnesseth unto the Father that he would be [aobedient](#note7a) unto him in keeping his commandments.

8 Wherefore, after he was baptized with water the Holy Ghost descended upon him in the [aform](#note8a) of a [bdove](#note8b).

9 And again, it showeth unto the children of men the straitness of the path, and the narrowness of the [agate](#note9a), by which they should enter, he having set the [bexample](#note9b) before them.

10 And he said unto the children of men: [aFollow](#note10a) thou me. Wherefore, my beloved brethren, can we [bfollow](#note10b) Jesus save we shall be willing to keep the commandments of the Father?

11 And the Father said: Repent ye, repent ye, and be baptized in the name of my Beloved Son.

12 And also, the voice of the Son came unto me, saying: He that is baptized in my name, to him will the Father [agive](#note12a) the Holy Ghost, like unto me; wherefore, [bfollow](#note12b) me, and do the things which ye have seen me do.

13 Wherefore, my beloved brethren, I know that if ye shall [afollow](#note13a) the Son, with full purpose of heart, acting no [bhypocrisy](#note13b) and no deception before God, but with real [cintent](#note13c), repenting of your sins, witnessing unto the Father that ye are [dwilling](#note13d) to take upon you the [ename](#note13e) of Christ, by [fbaptism](#note13f)—yea, by following your Lord and your Savior down into the water, according to his word, behold, then shall ye receive the Holy Ghost; yea, then cometh the [gbaptism of fire](#note13g) and of the Holy Ghost; and then can ye speak with the [htongue](#note13h) of angels, and shout praises unto the Holy One of Israel.

14 But, behold, my beloved brethren, thus came the voice of the Son unto me, saying: After ye have repented of your sins, and witnessed unto the Father that ye are willing to keep my commandments, by the baptism of water, and have received the baptism of fire and of the Holy Ghost, and can speak with a new tongue, yea, even with the tongue of angels, and after this should [adeny](#note14a) me, it would have been [bbetter](#note14b) for you that ye had not known me.

15 And I heard a voice from the Father, saying: Yea, the [awords](#note15a) of my Beloved are true and faithful. He that [bendureth](#note15b) to the [cend](#note15c), the same shall be saved.

16 And now, my beloved brethren, I know by this that unless a man shall [aendure](#note16a) to the end, in following the [bexample](#note16b) of the Son of the living God, he cannot be saved.

17 Wherefore, do the things which I have told you I have seen that your Lord and your Redeemer should do; for, for this cause have they been shown unto me, that ye might know the gate by which ye should enter. For the gate by which ye should enter is repentance and [abaptism](#note17a) by water; and then cometh a [bremission](#note17b) of your sins by fire and by the Holy Ghost.

18 And then are ye in this [astrait](#note18a) and narrow [bpath](#note18b) which leads to eternal life; yea, ye have entered in by the gate; ye have done according to the commandments of the Father and the Son; and ye have received the Holy Ghost, which [cwitnesses](#note18c) of the [dFather](#note18d) and the Son, unto the fulfilling of the promise which he hath made, that if ye entered in by the way ye should receive.

19 And now, my beloved brethren, after ye have gotten into this strait and narrow [apath](#note19a), I would ask if all is [bdone](#note19b)? Behold, I say unto you, Nay; for ye have not come thus far save it were by the word of Christ with unshaken [cfaith](#note19c) in him, [drelying](#note19d) wholly upon the merits of him who is mighty to [esave](#note19e).

20 Wherefore, ye must press forward with a [asteadfastness](#note20a) in Christ, having a perfect brightness of [bhope](#note20b), and a [clove](#note20c) of God and of all men. Wherefore, if ye shall press forward, feasting upon the word of Christ, and [dendure](#note20d) to the end, behold, thus saith the Father: Ye shall have [eeternal life](#note20e).

21 And now, behold, my beloved brethren, this is the [away](#note21a); and there is [bnone](#note21b) other way nor [cname](#note21c) given under heaven whereby man can be saved in the kingdom of God. And now, behold, this is the [ddoctrine](#note21d) of Christ, and the only and true doctrine of the [eFather](#note21e), and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost, which is [fone God](#note21f), without end. Amen.

Chapter 32

Angels speak by the power of the Holy Ghost—Men must pray and gain knowledge for themselves from the Holy Ghost. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And now, behold, my beloved brethren, I suppose that ye ponder somewhat in your hearts concerning that which ye should do after ye have entered in by the way. But, behold, why do ye ponder these things in your hearts?

2 Do ye not remember that I said unto you that after ye had [areceived](#note2a) the Holy Ghost ye could speak with the [btongue](#note2b) of angels? And now, how could ye speak with the tongue of angels save it were by the Holy Ghost?

3 [aAngels](#note3a) speak by the power of the Holy Ghost; wherefore, they speak the words of Christ. Wherefore, I said unto you, [bfeast](#note3b) upon the [cwords](#note3c) of Christ; for behold, the words of Christ will [dtell](#note3d) you all things what ye should do.

4 Wherefore, now after I have spoken these words, if ye cannot understand them it will be because ye [aask](#note4a) not, neither do ye knock; wherefore, ye are not brought into the light, but must perish in the dark.

5 For behold, again I say unto you that if ye will enter in by the way, and receive the Holy Ghost, it will [ashow](#note5a) unto you all things what ye should do.

6 Behold, this is the doctrine of Christ, and there will be no more doctrine given until after he shall [amanifest](#note6a) himself unto you in the flesh. And when he shall manifest himself unto you in the flesh, the things which he shall say unto you shall ye observe to do.

7 And now I, Nephi, cannot say more; the Spirit stoppeth mine utterance, and I am left to mourn because of the [aunbelief](#note7a), and the wickedness, and the ignorance, and the [bstiffneckedness](#note7b) of men; for they will [cnot](#note7c) search [dknowledge](#note7d), nor understand great knowledge, when it is given unto them in [eplainness](#note7e), even as plain as word can be.

8 And now, my beloved brethren, I perceive that ye ponder still in your hearts; and it grieveth me that I must speak concerning this thing. For if ye would hearken unto the [aSpirit](#note8a) which teacheth a man to [bpray](#note8b), ye would know that ye must [cpray](#note8c); for the [devil spirit](#note8d) teacheth not a man to pray, but teacheth him that he must not pray.

9 But behold, I say unto you that ye must [apray](#note9a) always, and not faint; that ye must not perform any thing unto the Lord save in the first place ye shall [bpray](#note9b) unto the Father in the [cname](#note9c) of Christ, that he will [dconsecrate](#note9d) thy performance unto thee, that thy performance may be for the [ewelfare](#note9e) of thy soul.

Chapter 33

Nephi’s words are true—They testify of Christ—Those who believe in Christ will believe Nephi’s words, which will stand as a witness before the judgment bar. About 559–545 B.C.

1 And now I, Nephi, cannot write all the things which were taught among my people; neither am I [amighty](#note1a) in writing, like unto speaking; for when a man [bspeaketh](#note1b) by the power of the Holy Ghost the power of the Holy Ghost carrieth it unto the hearts of the children of men.

2 But behold, there are many that [aharden](#note2a) their [bhearts](#note2b) against the [cHoly Spirit](#note2c), that it hath no place in them; wherefore, they cast many things away which are written and esteem them as things of naught.

3 But I, Nephi, have written what I have written, and I esteem it as of great [aworth](#note3a), and especially unto my people. For I [bpray](#note3b) continually for them by day, and mine [ceyes](#note3c) water my pillow by night, because of them; and I cry unto my God in faith, and I know that he will hear my cry.

4 And I know that the Lord God will consecrate my prayers for the gain of my people. And the words which I have written in weakness will be made strong unto them; for it [apersuadeth](#note4a) them to do good; it maketh known unto them of their fathers; and it speaketh of Jesus, and persuadeth them to believe in him, and to endure to the end, which is life [beternal](#note4b).

5 And it speaketh [aharshly](#note5a) against sin, according to the [bplainness](#note5b) of the truth; wherefore, no man will be angry at the words which I have written save he shall be of the spirit of the devil.

6 I [aglory](#note6a) in [bplainness](#note6b); I glory in truth; I glory in my Jesus, for he hath [credeemed](#note6c) my soul from hell.

7 I have [acharity](#note7a) for my people, and great faith in Christ that I shall meet many souls spotless at his judgment-seat.

8 I have charity for the [aJew](#note8a)—I say Jew, because I mean them from whence I came.

9 I also have charity for the Gentiles. But behold, for none of [athese](#note9a) can I hope except they shall be [breconciled](#note9b) unto Christ, and enter into the [cnarrow](#note9c) [dgate](#note9d), and [ewalk](#note9e) in the [fstrait](#note9f) path which leads to life, and continue in the path until the end of the day of [gprobation](#note9g).

10 And now, my beloved brethren, and also [aJew](#note10a), and all ye ends of the earth, hearken unto these words and [bbelieve](#note10b) in Christ; and if ye believe not in these words believe in Christ. And if ye shall [cbelieve](#note10c) in Christ ye will believe in these [dwords](#note10d), for they are the [ewords](#note10e) of Christ, and he hath given them unto me; and they [fteach](#note10f) all men that they should do good.

11 And if they are not the words of Christ, judge ye—for Christ will show unto you, with [apower](#note11a) and great [bglory](#note11b), that they are his words, at the last day; and you and I shall stand face to face before his bar; and ye shall know that I have been commanded of him to write these things, notwithstanding my weakness.

12 And I pray the Father in the name of Christ that many of us, if not all, may be saved in his [akingdom](#note12a) at that great and last day.

13 And now, my beloved brethren, all those who are of the house of Israel, and all ye ends of the earth, I speak unto you as the voice of one [acrying](#note13a) from the dust: Farewell until that great day shall come.

14 And you that will not partake of the goodness of God, and respect the words of the [aJews](#note14a), and also my [bwords](#note14b), and the words which shall proceed forth out of the mouth of the Lamb of God, behold, I bid you an everlasting farewell, for these words shall [ccondemn](#note14c) you at the last day.

15 For what I seal on earth, shall be brought against you at the [ajudgment](#note15a) bar; for thus hath the Lord commanded me, and I must [bobey](#note15b). Amen.

# The Book of Jacob the Brother of Nephi

The words of his preaching unto his brethren. He confoundeth a man who seeketh to overthrow the doctrine of Christ. A few words concerning the history of the people of Nephi.

Chapter 1

Jacob and Joseph seek to persuade men to believe in Christ and keep His commandments—Nephi dies—Wickedness prevails among the Nephites. About 544–421 B.C.

1 For behold, it came to pass that fifty and five years had passed away from the time that Lehi left Jerusalem; wherefore, Nephi gave me, Jacob, a [acommandment](#note1a) concerning the [bsmall](#note1b) plates, upon which these things are engraven.

2 And he gave me, Jacob, a commandment that I should [awrite](#note2a) upon [bthese plates](#note2b) a few of the things which I considered to be most precious; that I should not touch, save it were lightly, concerning the history of this people which are called the people of Nephi.

3 For he said that the history of his people should be engraven upon his [aother plates](#note3a), and that I should [bpreserve](#note3b) these plates and hand them down unto my seed, from generation to generation.

4 And if there were preaching which was [asacred](#note4a), or revelation which was great, or prophesying, that I should engraven the [bheads](#note4b) of them upon these plates, and touch upon them as much as it were possible, for Christ’s sake, and for the sake of our people.

5 For because of faith and great anxiety, it truly had been made manifest unto us concerning our people, what things should [ahappen](#note5a) unto them.

6 And we also had many revelations, and the spirit of much prophecy; wherefore, we knew of [aChrist](#note6a) and his kingdom, which should come.

7 Wherefore we labored diligently among our people, that we might persuade them to [acome](#note7a) unto Christ, and partake of the goodness of God, that they might enter into his [brest](#note7b), lest by any means he should swear in his wrath they should not [center](#note7c) in, as in the [dprovocation](#note7d) in the days of temptation while the children of Israel were in the [ewilderness](#note7e).

8 Wherefore, we would to God that we could persuade all men [anot to rebel](#note8a) against God, to [bprovoke](#note8b) him to anger, but that all men would believe in Christ, and view his death, and suffer his [ccross](#note8c) and bear the shame of the world; wherefore, I, Jacob, take it upon me to fulfil the commandment of my brother Nephi.

9 Now Nephi began to be old, and he saw that he must soon [adie](#note9a); wherefore, he [banointed](#note9b) a man to be a king and a ruler over his people now, according to the reigns of the [ckings](#note9c).

10 The people having loved Nephi exceedingly, he having been a great protector for them, having wielded the [asword](#note10a) of Laban in their defence, and having labored in all his days for their welfare—

11 Wherefore, the people were desirous to retain in remembrance his name. And whoso should reign in his stead were called by the people, second Nephi, third Nephi, and so forth, according to the reigns of the kings; and thus they were called by the people, let them be of whatever name they would.

12 And it came to pass that Nephi died.

13 Now the people which were not [aLamanites](#note13a) were Nephites; nevertheless, they were called Nephites, Jacobites, Josephites, [bZoramites](#note13b), Lamanites, Lemuelites, and Ishmaelites.

14 But I, Jacob, shall not hereafter distinguish [athem](#note14a) by these names, but I shall [bcall](#note14b) them Lamanites that seek to destroy the people of Nephi, and those who are friendly to Nephi I shall call [cNephites](#note14c), or the [dpeople](#note14d) of Nephi, according to the reigns of the kings.

15 And now it came to pass that the people of Nephi, under the reign of the second king, began to grow hard in their hearts, and indulge themselves somewhat in wicked practices, such as like unto David of old desiring many [awives](#note15a) and [bconcubines](#note15b), and also Solomon, his son.

16 Yea, and they also began to search much [agold](#note16a) and silver, and began to be lifted up somewhat in pride.

17 Wherefore I, Jacob, gave unto them these words as I taught them in the [atemple](#note17a), having first obtained mine [berrand](#note17b) from the Lord.

18 For I, Jacob, and my brother Joseph had been [aconsecrated](#note18a) priests and [bteachers](#note18b) of this people, by the hand of Nephi.

19 And we did [amagnify](#note19a) our office unto the Lord, taking upon us the [bresponsibility](#note19b), answering the sins of the people upon our own heads if we did not [cteach](#note19c) them the word of God with all diligence; wherefore, by laboring with our might their [dblood](#note19d) might not come upon our garments; otherwise their blood would come upon our garments, and we would not be found spotless at the last day.

Chapter 2

Jacob denounces the love of riches, pride, and unchastity—Men may seek riches to help their fellowmen—The Lord commands that no man among the Nephites may have more than one wife—The Lord delights in the chastity of women. About 544–421 B.C.

1 The [awords](#note1a) which Jacob, the brother of Nephi, spake unto the people of Nephi, after the death of Nephi:

2 Now, my beloved brethren, I, Jacob, according to the [aresponsibility](#note2a) which I am under to God, to [bmagnify](#note2b) mine office with [csoberness](#note2c), and that I might [drid](#note2d) my garments of your sins, I come up into the temple this day that I might declare unto you the word of God.

3 And ye yourselves know that I have hitherto been diligent in the office of my calling; but I this day am weighed down with much more desire and anxiety for the [awelfare](#note3a) of your souls than I have hitherto been.

4 For behold, as yet, ye have been obedient unto the word of the Lord, which I have given unto you.

5 But behold, hearken ye unto me, and know that by the help of the all-powerful Creator of heaven and earth I can tell you concerning your [athoughts](#note5a), how that ye are beginning to labor in sin, which sin appeareth very abominable unto me, yea, and abominable unto God.

6 Yea, it grieveth my soul and causeth me to shrink with shame before the presence of my Maker, that I must testify unto you concerning the wickedness of your hearts.

7 And also it grieveth me that I must use so much [aboldness](#note7a) of speech concerning you, before your wives and your children, many of whose feelings are exceedingly tender and [bchaste](#note7b) and delicate before God, which thing is pleasing unto God;

8 And it supposeth me that they have come up hither to hear the pleasing [aword of God](#note8a), yea, the word which healeth the wounded soul.

9 Wherefore, it burdeneth my soul that I should be constrained, because of the strict commandment which I have received from God, to [aadmonish](#note9a) you according to your crimes, to enlarge the wounds of those who are already wounded, instead of consoling and healing their wounds; and those who have not been wounded, instead of feasting upon the pleasing word of God have daggers placed to pierce their souls and wound their delicate minds.

10 But, notwithstanding the greatness of the task, I must do according to the strict [acommands](#note10a) of God, and tell you concerning your wickedness and abominations, in the presence of the pure in heart, and the broken heart, and under the glance of the [bpiercing](#note10b) eye of the Almighty God.

11 Wherefore, I must tell you the truth according to the [aplainness](#note11a) of the [bword](#note11b) of God. For behold, as I inquired of the Lord, thus came the word unto me, saying: Jacob, get thou up into the temple on the morrow, and declare the word which I shall give thee unto this people.

12 And now behold, my brethren, this is the word which I declare unto you, that many of you have begun to search for gold, and for silver, and for all manner of precious [aores](#note12a), in the which this land, which is a [bland of promise](#note12b) unto you and to your seed, doth abound most plentifully.

13 And the hand of providence hath smiled upon you most pleasingly, that you have obtained many riches; and because some of you have obtained more abundantly than that of your brethren ye are [alifted up](#note13a) in the pride of your hearts, and wear stiff necks and high heads because of the costliness of your apparel, and persecute your brethren because ye suppose that ye are better than they.

14 And now, my brethren, do ye suppose that God justifieth you in this thing? Behold, I say unto you, Nay. But he condemneth you, and if ye persist in these things his judgments must speedily come unto you.

15 O that he would show you that he can pierce you, and with one glance of his [aeye](#note15a) he can smite you to the dust!

16 O that he would rid you from this iniquity and abomination. And, O that ye would listen unto the word of his commands, and let not this [apride](#note16a) of your hearts destroy your souls!

17 Think of your [abrethren](#note17a) like unto yourselves, and be familiar with all and free with your [bsubstance](#note17b), that [cthey](#note17c) may be rich like unto you.

18 But [abefore](#note18a) ye seek for [briches](#note18b), seek ye for the [ckingdom](#note18c) of God.

19 And after ye have obtained a hope in Christ ye shall obtain riches, if ye seek them; and ye will seek them for the intent to [ado](#note19a) good—to clothe the naked, and to feed the hungry, and to liberate the captive, and administer relief to the sick and the afflicted.

20 And now, my brethren, I have spoken unto you concerning pride; and those of you which have afflicted your neighbor, and persecuted him because ye were proud in your hearts, of the things which God hath given you, what say ye of it?

21 Do ye not suppose that such things are abominable unto him who created all flesh? And the one being is as precious in his sight as the other. And all flesh is of the dust; and for the selfsame end hath he created them, that they should keep his [acommandments](#note21a) and glorify him forever.

22 And now I make an end of speaking unto you concerning this pride. And were it not that I must speak unto you concerning a grosser crime, my heart would rejoice exceedingly because of you.

23 But the word of God burdens me because of your grosser crimes. For behold, thus saith the Lord: This people begin to wax in iniquity; they understand not the scriptures, for they seek to excuse themselves in committing [awhoredoms](#note23a), because of the things which were written concerning David, and Solomon his son.

24 Behold, David and [aSolomon](#note24a) truly had many [bwives](#note24b) and concubines, which thing was [cabominable](#note24c) before me, saith the Lord.

25 Wherefore, thus saith the Lord, I have led this people forth out of the land of Jerusalem, by the power of mine arm, that I might raise up unto me a [arighteous](#note25a) branch from the fruit of the loins of Joseph.

26 Wherefore, I the Lord God will not suffer that this people shall do like unto them of old.

27 Wherefore, my brethren, hear me, and hearken to the word of the Lord: For there shall not any [aman](#note27a) among you have save it be [bone](#note27b) [cwife](#note27c); and concubines he shall have none;

28 For I, the Lord God, delight in the [achastity](#note28a) of women. And [bwhoredoms](#note28b) are an abomination before me; thus saith the Lord of Hosts.

29 Wherefore, this people shall keep my commandments, saith the Lord of Hosts, or [acursed](#note29a) be the land for their sakes.

30 For if I will, saith the Lord of Hosts, raise up [aseed](#note30a) unto me, I will command my people; otherwise they shall hearken unto these things.

31 For behold, I, the Lord, have seen the sorrow, and heard the mourning of the daughters of my people in the land of Jerusalem, yea, and in all the lands of my people, because of the wickedness and [aabominations](#note31a) of their [bhusbands](#note31b).

32 And I will not suffer, saith the Lord of Hosts, that the cries of the fair daughters of this people, which I have led out of the land of Jerusalem, shall come up unto me against the men of my people, saith the Lord of Hosts.

33 For they shall not lead away captive the daughters of my people because of their tenderness, save I shall visit them with a sore curse, even unto destruction; for they shall not commit [awhoredoms](#note33a), like unto them of old, saith the Lord of Hosts.

34 And now behold, my brethren, ye know that these commandments were given to our [afather](#note34a), Lehi; wherefore, ye have known them before; and ye have come unto great condemnation; for ye have done these things which ye ought not to have done.

35 Behold, ye have done [agreater](#note35a) iniquities than the Lamanites, our brethren. Ye have broken the hearts of your tender wives, and lost the confidence of your children, because of your bad examples before them; and the sobbings of their hearts ascend up to God against you. And because of the [bstrictness](#note35b) of the word of God, which cometh down against you, many hearts died, pierced with deep wounds.

Chapter 3

The pure in heart receive the pleasing word of God—Lamanite righteousness exceeds that of the Nephites—Jacob warns against fornication, lasciviousness, and every sin. About 544–421 B.C.

1 But behold, I, Jacob, would speak unto you that are pure in heart. Look unto God with firmness of mind, and [apray](#note1a) unto him with exceeding faith, and he will [bconsole](#note1b) you in your [cafflictions](#note1c), and he will plead your cause, and send down [djustice](#note1d) upon those who seek your destruction.

2 O all ye that are pure in heart, lift up your heads and receive the pleasing word of God, and feast upon his [alove](#note2a); for ye may, if your [bminds](#note2b) are [cfirm](#note2c), forever.

3 But, wo, wo, unto you that are not pure in heart, that are filthy this day before God; for except ye repent the land is [acursed](#note3a) for your sakes; and the Lamanites, which are not [bfilthy](#note3b) like unto you, nevertheless they are [ccursed](#note3c) with a sore cursing, shall scourge you even unto destruction.

4 And the time speedily cometh, that except ye repent they shall possess the land of your inheritance, and the Lord God will [alead](#note4a) away the righteous out from among you.

5 Behold, the Lamanites your brethren, whom ye hate because of their filthiness and the cursing which hath come upon their skins, are more righteous than you; for they have not [aforgotten](#note5a) the commandment of the Lord, which was given unto our father—that they should have save it were [bone](#note5b) wife, and [cconcubines](#note5c) they should have none, and there should not be [dwhoredoms](#note5d) committed among them.

6 And now, this commandment they observe to keep; wherefore, because of this observance, in keeping this commandment, the Lord God will not destroy them, but will be [amerciful](#note6a) unto them; and one day they shall [bbecome](#note6b) a blessed people.

7 Behold, their [ahusbands](#note7a) [blove](#note7b) their [cwives](#note7c), and their wives love their husbands; and their husbands and their wives love their children; and their [dunbelief](#note7d) and their hatred towards you is because of the iniquity of their fathers; wherefore, how much better are you than they, in the sight of your great Creator?

8 O my brethren, I fear that unless ye shall repent of your sins that their skins will be [awhiter](#note8a) than yours, when ye shall be brought with them before the throne of God.

9 Wherefore, a commandment I give unto you, which is the word of God, that ye [arevile](#note9a) no more against them because of the darkness of their skins; neither shall ye revile against them because of their filthiness; but ye shall remember your own filthiness, and remember that their filthiness came because of their fathers.

10 Wherefore, ye shall remember your [achildren](#note10a), how that ye have grieved their hearts because of the [bexample](#note10b) that ye have set before them; and also, remember that ye may, because of your filthiness, bring your children unto destruction, and their sins be heaped upon your heads at the last day.

11 O my brethren, hearken unto my words; [aarouse](#note11a) the faculties of your souls; shake yourselves that ye may [bawake](#note11b) from the slumber of death; and loose yourselves from the pains of [chell](#note11c) that ye may not become [dangels](#note11d) to the devil, to be cast into that lake of fire and brimstone which is the second [edeath](#note11e).

12 And now I, Jacob, spake many more things unto the people of Nephi, [awarning](#note12a) them against [bfornication](#note12b) and [clasciviousness](#note12c), and every kind of sin, telling them the awful consequences of them.

13 And a hundredth part of the proceedings of this people, which now began to be numerous, cannot be written upon [athese plates](#note13a); but many of their proceedings are written upon the [blarger plates](#note13b), and their wars, and their contentions, and the reigns of their kings.

14 [aThese](#note14a) plates are called the plates of Jacob, and they were [bmade](#note14b) by the hand of Nephi. And I make an end of speaking these words.
