  b

  TG Faith.

Chapter 32

Alma teaches the poor whose afflictions had humbled them—Faith is a hope in that which is not seen which is true—Alma testifies that angels minister to men, women, and children—Alma compares the word unto a seed—It must be planted and nourished—Then it grows into a tree from which the fruit of eternal life is picked. About 74 B.C.

1 And it came to pass that they did go forth, and began to preach the word of God unto the people, entering into their synagogues, and into their houses; yea, and even they did preach the word in their streets.

2 And it came to pass that after much labor among them, they began to have success among the [apoor](#note2a) class of people; for behold, they were cast out of the synagogues because of the coarseness of their apparel—

3 Therefore they were not permitted to enter into their synagogues to worship God, being esteemed as filthiness; therefore they were poor; yea, they were esteemed by their brethren as [adross](#note3a); therefore they were [bpoor](#note3b) as to things of the world; and also they were poor in heart.

4 Now, as Alma was teaching and speaking unto the people upon the hill [aOnidah](#note4a), there came a great [bmultitude](#note4b) unto him, who were those of whom we have been speaking, of whom were [cpoor in heart](#note4c), because of their poverty as to the things of the world.

5 And they came unto Alma; and the one who was the foremost among them said unto him: Behold, [awhat](#note5a) shall these my brethren do, for they are [bdespised](#note5b) of all men because of their poverty, yea, and more especially by our priests; for they have [ccast](#note5c) us out of our synagogues which we have labored abundantly to build with our own hands; and they have cast us out because of our exceeding poverty; and we have [dno](#note5d) place to worship our God; and behold, [ewhat](#note5e) shall we do?

6 And now when Alma heard this, he turned him about, his face immediately towards him, and he beheld with great joy; for he beheld that their [aafflictions](#note6a) had truly [bhumbled](#note6b) them, and that they were in a [cpreparation](#note6c) to hear the word.

7 Therefore he did say no more to the other multitude; but he stretched forth his hand, and cried unto those whom he beheld, who were truly penitent, and said unto them:

8 I behold that ye are [alowly](#note8a) in heart; and if so, blessed are ye.

9 Behold thy brother hath said, What shall we do?—for we are cast out of our synagogues, that we cannot worship our God.

10 Behold I say unto you, do ye suppose that ye [acannot worship](#note10a) God save it be in your synagogues only?

11 And moreover, I would ask, do ye suppose that ye must not worship God only [aonce](#note11a) in a week?

12 I say unto you, it is well that ye are cast out of your synagogues, that ye may be humble, and that ye may learn [awisdom](#note12a); for it is necessary that ye should learn wisdom; for it is because that ye are cast out, that ye are despised of your brethren because of your exceeding [bpoverty](#note12b), that ye are brought to a lowliness of heart; for ye are necessarily brought to be humble.

13 And now, because ye are compelled to be humble blessed are ye; for a man sometimes, if he is compelled to be humble, seeketh [arepentance](#note13a); and now surely, whosoever repenteth shall find mercy; and he that findeth mercy and [bendureth](#note13b) to the end the same shall be saved.

14 And now, as I said unto you, that because ye were compelled to be [ahumble](#note14a) ye were blessed, do ye not suppose that they are more blessed who truly humble themselves because of the word?

15 Yea, he that truly humbleth himself, and repenteth of his sins, and endureth to the end, the same shall be blessed—yea, much more blessed than they who are compelled to be humble because of their exceeding poverty.

16 Therefore, blessed are they who [ahumble](#note16a) themselves without being [bcompelled](#note16b) to be humble; or rather, in other words, blessed is he that believeth in the word of God, and is baptized without [cstubbornness](#note16c) of heart, yea, without being brought to know the word, or even compelled to know, before they will believe.

17 Yea, there are many who do say: If thou wilt show unto us a [asign](#note17a) from heaven, then we shall know of a surety; then we shall believe.

18 Now I ask, is this faith? Behold, I say unto you, Nay; for if a man knoweth a thing he hath no cause to [abelieve](#note18a), for he knoweth it.

19 And now, how much [amore](#note19a) [bcursed](#note19b) is he that [cknoweth](#note19c) the [dwill](#note19d) of God and doeth it not, than he that only believeth, or only hath cause to believe, and falleth into [etransgression](#note19e)?

20 Now of this thing ye must judge. Behold, I say unto you, that it is on the one hand even as it is on the other; and it shall be unto every man according to his work.

21 And now as I said concerning faith—[afaith](#note21a) is not to have a perfect knowledge of things; therefore if ye have faith ye [bhope](#note21b) for things which are [cnot](#note21c) seen, which are true.

22 And now, behold, I say unto you, and I would that ye should remember, that God is [amerciful](#note22a) unto all who believe on his name; therefore he desireth, in the first place, that ye should believe, yea, even on his word.

23 And now, he imparteth his word by angels unto men, yea, [anot](#note23a) only men but women also. Now this is not all; little [bchildren](#note23b) do have words given unto them many times, which [cconfound](#note23c) the wise and the learned.

24 And now, my beloved brethren, as ye have desired to know of me what ye shall do because ye are afflicted and cast out—now I do not desire that ye should suppose that I mean to judge you only according to that which is true—

25 For I do not mean that ye all of you have been compelled to humble yourselves; for I verily believe that there are some among you who [awould humble](#note25a) themselves, let them be in whatsoever circumstances they might.

26 Now, as I said concerning faith—that it was not a perfect knowledge—even so it is with my words. Ye cannot know of their surety at first, unto perfection, any more than faith is a perfect knowledge.

27 But behold, if ye will awake and arouse your faculties, even to an experiment upon my words, and exercise a particle of faith, yea, even if ye can no more than [adesire](#note27a) to believe, let this desire work in you, even until ye believe in a manner that ye can give place for a portion of my words.

28 Now, we will compare the word unto a [aseed](#note28a). Now, if ye give place, that a [bseed](#note28b) may be planted in your [cheart](#note28c), behold, if it be a true seed, or a good seed, if ye do not cast it out by your [dunbelief](#note28d), that ye will resist the Spirit of the Lord, behold, it will begin to swell within your breasts; and when you feel these swelling motions, ye will begin to say within yourselves—It must needs be that this is a good seed, or that the word is good, for it beginneth to enlarge my soul; yea, it beginneth to [eenlighten](#note28e) my [funderstanding](#note28f), yea, it beginneth to be delicious to me.

29 Now behold, would not this increase your faith? I say unto you, Yea; nevertheless it hath not grown up to a perfect knowledge.

30 But behold, as the seed swelleth, and sprouteth, and beginneth to grow, then you must needs say that the seed is good; for behold it swelleth, and sprouteth, and beginneth to grow. And now, behold, will not this strengthen your faith? Yea, it will strengthen your faith: for ye will say I know that this is a good seed; for behold it sprouteth and beginneth to grow.

31 And now, behold, are ye sure that this is a good seed? I say unto you, Yea; for every seed bringeth forth unto its own [alikeness](#note31a).

32 Therefore, if a seed groweth it is good, but if it groweth not, behold it is not good, therefore it is cast away.

33 And now, behold, because ye have tried the experiment, and planted the seed, and it swelleth and sprouteth, and beginneth to grow, ye must needs know that the seed is good.

34 And now, behold, is your [aknowledge](#note34a) [bperfect](#note34b)? Yea, your knowledge is perfect in that thing, and your [cfaith](#note34c) is dormant; and this because you know, for ye know that the word hath swelled your souls, and ye also know that it hath sprouted up, that your understanding doth begin to be enlightened, and your [dmind](#note34d) doth begin to expand.

35 O then, is not this real? I say unto you, Yea, because it is [alight](#note35a); and whatsoever is light, is [bgood](#note35b), because it is discernible, therefore ye must know that it is good; and now behold, after ye have tasted this light is your knowledge perfect?

36 Behold I say unto you, Nay; neither must ye lay aside your faith, for ye have only exercised your faith to plant the seed that ye might try the experiment to know if the seed was good.

37 And behold, as the tree beginneth to grow, ye will say: Let us nourish it with great care, that it may get root, that it may grow up, and bring forth fruit unto us. And now behold, if ye nourish it with much care it will get root, and grow up, and bring forth fruit.

38 But if ye [aneglect](#note38a) the tree, and take no thought for its nourishment, behold it will not get any root; and when the heat of the sun cometh and scorcheth it, because it hath no root it withers away, and ye pluck it up and cast it out.

39 Now, this is not because the seed was not good, neither is it because the fruit thereof would not be desirable; but it is because your [aground](#note39a) is [bbarren](#note39b), and ye will not nourish the tree, therefore ye cannot have the fruit thereof.

40 And thus, if ye will not nourish the word, looking forward with an eye of faith to the fruit thereof, ye can never pluck of the fruit of the [atree of life](#note40a).

41 But if ye will nourish the word, yea, nourish the tree as it beginneth to grow, by your faith with great diligence, and with [apatience](#note41a), looking forward to the fruit thereof, it shall take root; and behold it shall be a tree [bspringing up](#note41b) unto everlasting life.

42 And because of your [adiligence](#note42a) and your faith and your patience with the word in nourishing it, that it may take root in you, behold, by and by ye shall pluck the [bfruit](#note42b) thereof, which is most precious, which is sweet above all that is sweet, and which is white above all that is white, yea, and pure above all that is pure; and ye shall feast upon this fruit even until ye are filled, that ye hunger not, neither shall ye thirst.

43 Then, my brethren, ye shall [areap](#note43a) the [brewards](#note43b) of your faith, and your diligence, and patience, and long-suffering, waiting for the tree to bring forth [cfruit](#note43c) unto you.

  c

  [Alma 33:23](../alma/33.xhtml#p23).

Chapter 33

Zenos taught that men should pray and worship in all places, and that judgments are turned away because of the Son—Zenock taught that mercy is bestowed because of the Son—Moses had lifted up in the wilderness a type of the Son of God. About 74 B.C.

1 Now after Alma had spoken these words, they sent forth unto him desiring to know whether they should believe in [aone God](#note1a), that they might obtain this fruit of which he had spoken, or [bhow](#note1b) they should plant the [cseed](#note1c), or the word of which he had spoken, which he said must be planted in their hearts; or in what manner they should begin to exercise their faith.

2 And Alma said unto them: Behold, ye have said that ye [acould](#note2a) not [bworship](#note2b) your God because ye are cast out of your synagogues. But behold, I say unto you, if ye suppose that ye cannot worship God, ye do greatly err, and ye ought to search the [cscriptures](#note2c); if ye suppose that they have taught you this, ye do not understand them.

3 Do ye remember to have read what [aZenos](#note3a), the prophet of old, has said concerning prayer or [bworship](#note3b)?

4 For he said: Thou art merciful, O God, for thou hast heard my prayer, even when I was [ain](#note4a) the wilderness; yea, thou wast merciful when I prayed concerning those who were mine [benemies](#note4b), and thou didst turn them to me.

5 Yea, O God, and thou wast merciful unto me when I did cry unto thee in my [afield](#note5a); when I did cry unto thee in my prayer, and thou didst hear me.

6 And again, O God, when I did turn to my house thou didst hear me in my prayer.

7 And when I did turn unto my [acloset](#note7a), O Lord, and prayed unto thee, thou didst hear me.

8 Yea, thou art merciful unto thy children when they cry unto thee, to be heard of thee and not of men, and thou [awilt hear](#note8a) them.

9 Yea, O God, thou hast been merciful unto me, and heard my cries in the midst of thy congregations.

10 Yea, and thou hast also heard me when I have been [acast out](#note10a) and have been despised by mine enemies; yea, thou didst hear my cries, and wast angry with mine enemies, and thou didst [bvisit](#note10b) them in thine anger with speedy destruction.

11 And thou didst hear me because of mine afflictions and my [asincerity](#note11a); and it is because of thy Son that thou hast been thus merciful unto me, therefore I will cry unto thee in all mine [bafflictions](#note11b), for in thee is my joy; for thou hast turned thy judgments away from me, [cbecause](#note11c) of thy Son.

12 And now Alma said unto them: Do ye [abelieve](#note12a) those scriptures which have been written by them of old?

13 Behold, if ye do, ye must believe what [aZenos](#note13a) said; for, behold he said: Thou hast turned away thy judgments because of thy Son.

14 Now behold, my brethren, I would ask if ye have read the scriptures? If ye have, how can ye [adisbelieve](#note14a) on the Son of God?

15 For it is [anot](#note15a) written that Zenos alone spake of these things, but [bZenock](#note15b) also spake of these things—

16 For behold, he said: Thou art angry, O Lord, with this people, because they [awill](#note16a) not understand thy mercies which thou hast bestowed upon them because of thy Son.

17 And now, my brethren, ye see that a second prophet of old has testified of the Son of God, and because the people would not understand his words they [astoned](#note17a) him to death.

18 But behold, this is not all; these are not the only ones who have spoken concerning the Son of God.

19 Behold, he was spoken of by [aMoses](#note19a); yea, and behold a [btype](#note19b) was [craised](#note19c) up in the wilderness, that whosoever would look upon it might live. And many did look and live.

20 But few understood the meaning of those things, and this because of the hardness of their hearts. But there were many who were so hardened that they would not look, therefore they perished. Now the reason they would not look is because they did not believe that it would [aheal](#note20a) them.

21 O my brethren, if ye could be healed by merely casting about your eyes that ye might be healed, would ye not behold quickly, or would ye rather harden your hearts in [aunbelief](#note21a), and be [bslothful](#note21b), that ye would not cast about your eyes, that ye might perish?

22 If so, wo shall come upon you; but if not so, then cast about your eyes and [abegin](#note22a) to believe in the Son of God, that he will come to redeem his people, and that he shall suffer and die to [batone](#note22b) for their sins; and that he shall [crise](#note22c) again from the dead, which shall bring to pass the [dresurrection](#note22d), that all men shall stand before him, to be [ejudged](#note22e) at the last and judgment day, according to their [fworks](#note22f).

23 And now, my brethren, I desire that ye shall [aplant](#note23a) this word in your hearts, and as it beginneth to swell even so nourish it by your faith. And behold, it will become a tree, [bspringing up](#note23b) in you unto [ceverlasting life](#note23c). And then may God grant unto you that your [dburdens](#note23d) may be light, through the joy of his Son. And even all this can ye do if ye [ewill](#note23e). Amen.

Chapter 34

Amulek testifies that the word is in Christ unto salvation—Unless an atonement is made, all mankind must perish—The whole law of Moses points toward the sacrifice of the Son of God—The eternal plan of redemption is based on faith and repentance—Pray for temporal and spiritual blessings—This life is the time for men to prepare to meet God—Work out your salvation with fear before God. About 74 B.C.

1 And now it came to pass that after Alma had spoken these words unto them he sat down upon the ground, and [aAmulek](#note1a) arose and began to teach them, saying:

2 My brethren, I think that it is impossible that ye should be ignorant of the things which have been spoken concerning the coming of Christ, who is taught by us to be the Son of God; yea, I know that [athese things](#note2a) were taught unto you bountifully before your dissension from among us.

3 And as ye have desired of my beloved brother that he should make known unto you [awhat](#note3a) ye should do, because of your afflictions; and he hath spoken somewhat unto you to prepare your minds; yea, and he hath exhorted you unto faith and to patience—

4 Yea, even that ye would have so much faith as even to [aplant](#note4a) the word in your hearts, that ye may try the experiment of its goodness.

5 And we have beheld that the great question which is in your minds is whether the word be in the Son of God, or whether there shall be [ano](#note5a) Christ.

6 And ye also beheld that my brother has proved unto you, in many instances, that the [aword](#note6a) is in Christ unto salvation.

7 My brother has called upon the words of [aZenos](#note7a), that redemption cometh through the Son of God, and also upon the words of [bZenock](#note7b); and also he has appealed unto [cMoses](#note7c), to prove that these things are true.

8 And now, behold, I will [atestify](#note8a) unto you of myself that these things are true. Behold, I say unto you, that I do know that Christ shall come among the children of men, to take upon him the [btransgressions](#note8b) of his people, and that he shall [catone](#note8c) for the sins of the world; for the Lord God hath spoken it.

9 For it is expedient that an [aatonement](#note9a) should be made; for according to the great [bplan](#note9b) of the Eternal God there must be an atonement made, or else all mankind must unavoidably perish; yea, all are hardened; yea, all are [cfallen](#note9c) and are lost, and must perish except it be through the atonement which it is expedient should be made.

10 For it is expedient that there should be a great and last [asacrifice](#note10a); yea, not a [bsacrifice](#note10b) of man, neither of beast, neither of any manner of fowl; for it shall not be a human sacrifice; but it must be an [cinfinite](#note10c) and [deternal](#note10d) [esacrifice](#note10e).

11 Now there is not any man that can sacrifice his own blood which will atone for the sins of another. Now, if a man murdereth, behold will our law, which is [ajust](#note11a), take the life of his brother? I say unto you, Nay.

12 But the law requireth the [alife](#note12a) of him who hath [bmurdered](#note12b); therefore there can be nothing which is short of an infinite atonement which will suffice for the sins of the world.

13 Therefore, it is expedient that there should be a great and last sacrifice, and then shall there be, or it is expedient there should be, a [astop](#note13a) to the shedding of [bblood](#note13b); then shall the [claw](#note13c) of Moses be fulfilled; yea, it shall be all fulfilled, every jot and tittle, and none shall have passed away.

14 And behold, this is the whole [ameaning](#note14a) of the [blaw](#note14b), every whit [cpointing](#note14c) to that great and last [dsacrifice](#note14d); and that great and last [esacrifice](#note14e) will be the Son of God, yea, [finfinite](#note14f) and eternal.

15 And thus he shall bring [asalvation](#note15a) to all those who shall believe on his name; this being the intent of this last sacrifice, to bring about the bowels of mercy, which overpowereth justice, and bringeth about means unto men that they may have faith unto repentance.

16 And thus [amercy](#note16a) can satisfy the demands of [bjustice](#note16b), and encircles them in the arms of safety, while he that exercises no faith unto repentance is exposed to the whole law of the demands of [cjustice](#note16c); therefore only unto him that has faith unto repentance is brought about the great and eternal [dplan](#note16d) of [eredemption](#note16e).

17 Therefore may God grant unto you, my brethren, that ye may begin to exercise your [afaith](#note17a) unto repentance, that ye begin to [bcall](#note17b) upon his holy name, that he would have mercy upon you;

18 Yea, cry unto him for mercy; for he is [amighty](#note18a) to save.

19 Yea, humble yourselves, and continue in [aprayer](#note19a) unto him.

20 Cry unto him when ye are in your [afields](#note20a), yea, over all your flocks.

21 [aCry](#note21a) unto him in your houses, yea, over all your household, both morning, mid-day, and evening.

22 Yea, cry unto him against the power of your [aenemies](#note22a).

23 Yea, [acry](#note23a) unto him against the [bdevil](#note23b), who is an enemy to all [crighteousness](#note23c).

24 Cry unto him over the crops of your fields, that ye may prosper in them.

25 Cry over the flocks of your fields, that they may increase.

26 But this is not all; ye must [apour out](#note26a) your souls in your [bclosets](#note26b), and your secret places, and in your wilderness.

27 Yea, and when you do not cry unto the Lord, let your [ahearts](#note27a) be [bfull](#note27b), drawn out in prayer unto him continually for your [cwelfare](#note27c), and also for the welfare of [dthose](#note27d) who are around you.

28 And now behold, my beloved brethren, I say unto you, do not suppose that this is all; for after ye have done all these things, if ye [aturn away](#note28a) the [bneedy](#note28b), and the [cnaked](#note28c), and visit not the sick and afflicted, and [dimpart](#note28d) of your substance, if ye have, to those who stand in need—I say unto you, if ye do not any of these things, behold, your [eprayer](#note28e) is [fvain](#note28f), and availeth you nothing, and ye are as [ghypocrites](#note28g) who do deny the faith.

29 Therefore, if ye do not remember to be [acharitable](#note29a), ye are as dross, which the refiners do cast out, (it being of no worth) and is trodden under foot of men.

30 And now, my brethren, I would that, after ye have [areceived](#note30a) so many witnesses, seeing that the holy scriptures testify of these things, ye come forth and bring [bfruit](#note30b) unto repentance.

31 Yea, I would that ye would come forth and [aharden](#note31a) not your hearts any longer; for behold, now is the time and the [bday](#note31b) of your [csalvation](#note31c); and therefore, if ye will repent and [dharden](#note31d) not your hearts, immediately shall the great plan of redemption be brought about unto you.

32 For behold, this [alife](#note32a) is the time for men to [bprepare](#note32b) to meet God; yea, behold the day of [cthis](#note32c) life is the day for men to perform their [dlabors](#note32d).

33 And now, as I said unto you before, as ye have had so many [awitnesses](#note33a), therefore, I beseech of you that ye do not [bprocrastinate](#note33b) the day of your [crepentance](#note33c) until the end; for after this day of life, which is given us to prepare for eternity, behold, if we do not improve our time while in this life, then cometh the [dnight](#note33d) of [edarkness](#note33e) wherein there can be no labor performed.

34 Ye cannot say, when ye are brought to that awful [acrisis](#note34a), that I will repent, that I will return to my God. Nay, ye cannot say this; for that same spirit which doth [bpossess](#note34b) your bodies at the time that ye go out of this life, that same spirit will have power to possess your body in that eternal world.

35 For behold, if ye have procrastinated the day of your repentance even until death, behold, ye have become [asubjected](#note35a) to the spirit of the devil, and he doth [bseal](#note35b) you his; therefore, the Spirit of the Lord hath withdrawn from you, and hath no place in you, and the devil hath all power over you; and this is the final state of the wicked.

36 And this I know, because the Lord hath said he dwelleth not in [aunholy](#note36a) temples, but in the [bhearts](#note36b) of the [crighteous](#note36c) doth he dwell; yea, and he has also said that the righteous shall sit down in his kingdom, to go no more out; but their garments should be made white through the [dblood](#note36d) of the Lamb.

37 And now, my beloved brethren, I desire that ye should remember these things, and that ye should [awork](#note37a) out your salvation with fear before God, and that ye should no more deny the coming of Christ;

38 That ye [acontend](#note38a) no more against the Holy Ghost, but that ye receive it, and take upon you the [bname](#note38b) of Christ; that ye humble yourselves even to the dust, and [cworship](#note38c) God, in whatsoever place ye may be in, in spirit and in truth; and that ye live in [dthanksgiving](#note38d) daily, for the many [emercies](#note38e) and blessings which he doth bestow upon you.

39 Yea, and I also [aexhort](#note39a) you, my brethren, that ye be [bwatchful](#note39b) unto prayer continually, that ye may not be led away by the [ctemptations](#note39c) of the devil, that he may not overpower you, that ye may not become his subjects at the last day; for behold, he rewardeth you [dno](#note39d) good thing.

40 And now my beloved brethren, I would exhort you to have [apatience](#note40a), and that ye bear with all manner of [bafflictions](#note40b); that ye do not [crevile](#note40c) against those who do cast you out because of your [dexceeding](#note40d) poverty, lest ye become sinners like unto them;

41 But that ye have [apatience](#note41a), and bear with those [bafflictions](#note41b), with a firm hope that ye shall one day rest from all your afflictions.

Chapter 35

The preaching of the word destroys the craft of the Zoramites—They expel the converts, who then join the people of Ammon in Jershon—Alma sorrows because of the wickedness of the people. About 74 B.C.

1 Now it came to pass that after Amulek had made an end of these words, they withdrew themselves from the multitude and came over into the land of [aJershon](#note1a).

2 Yea, and the rest of the brethren, after they had preached the word unto the [aZoramites](#note2a), also came over into the land of Jershon.

3 And it came to pass that after the more popular part of the Zoramites had consulted together concerning the words which had been preached unto them, they were angry because of the word, for it did destroy their [acraft](#note3a); therefore they would not hearken unto the words.

4 And they sent and gathered together throughout all the land all the people, and consulted with them concerning the words which had been spoken.

5 Now their rulers and their priests and their teachers did not let the people know concerning their desires; therefore they found out privily the minds of all the people.

6 And it came to pass that after they had found out the minds of all the people, those who were in favor of the words which had been spoken by Alma and his brethren were cast out of the land; and they were [amany](#note6a); and they came over also into the land of Jershon.

7 And it came to pass that Alma and his brethren did minister unto them.

8 Now the people of the Zoramites were angry with the [apeople](#note8a) of Ammon who were in Jershon, and the [bchief](#note8b) ruler of the Zoramites, being a very wicked man, sent over unto the people of Ammon desiring them that they should cast out of their land all those who came over from them into their land.

9 And he breathed out many threatenings against them. And now the people of Ammon did not fear their words; therefore they did not cast them out, but they did receive all the poor of the Zoramites that came over unto them; and they did [anourish](#note9a) them, and did clothe them, and did give unto them lands for their inheritance; and they did administer unto them according to their wants.

10 Now this did [astir up](#note10a) the Zoramites to [banger](#note10b) against the people of Ammon, and they began to mix with the Lamanites and to stir them up also to anger against them.

11 And thus the Zoramites and the Lamanites began to make preparations for war against the people of Ammon, and also against the Nephites.

12 And thus ended the seventeenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi.

13 And the people of Ammon departed out of the land of Jershon, and came over into the land of Melek, and gave place in the land of Jershon for the armies of the Nephites, that they might contend with the armies of the Lamanites and the armies of the Zoramites; and thus commenced a war betwixt the Lamanites and the Nephites, in the eighteenth year of the reign of the judges; and an [aaccount](#note13a) shall be given of their wars hereafter.

14 And Alma, and Ammon, and their brethren, and also the [atwo](#note14a) sons of Alma returned to the land of Zarahemla, after having been instruments in the hands of God of bringing [bmany](#note14b) of the [cZoramites](#note14c) to repentance; and as many as were brought to repentance were driven out of their land; but they have lands for their inheritance in the land of Jershon, and they have taken up arms to defend themselves, and their wives, and children, and their lands.

15 Now Alma, being [agrieved](#note15a) for the iniquity of his people, yea for the wars, and the bloodsheds, and the contentions which were among them; and having been to declare the word, or sent to declare the word, among all the people in every city; and seeing that the hearts of the people began to wax hard, and that they began to be [boffended](#note15b) because of the strictness of the word, his heart was exceedingly sorrowful.

16 Therefore, he caused that his sons should be gathered together, that he might give unto them every one his [acharge](#note16a), separately, concerning the things pertaining unto righteousness. And we have an account of his commandments, which he gave unto them according to his own record.

The commandments of Alma to his son Helaman.

Comprising chapters 36 and 37.

Chapter 36

Alma testifies to Helaman of his conversion after seeing an angel—He suffered the pains of a damned soul; he called upon the name of Jesus, and was then born of God—Sweet joy filled his soul—He saw concourses of angels praising God—Many converts have tasted and seen as he has tasted and seen. About 74 B.C.

1 My [ason](#note1a), give ear to my words; for I swear unto you, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land.

2 I would that ye should do as I have done, in remembering the captivity of our fathers; for they were in [abondage](#note2a), and none could [bdeliver](#note2b) them except it was the [cGod](#note2c) of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob; and he surely did deliver them in their afflictions.

3 And now, O my son Helaman, behold, thou art in thy youth, and therefore, I beseech of thee that thou wilt hear my words and learn of me; for I do know that whosoever shall put their [atrust](#note3a) in God shall be supported in their [btrials](#note3b), and their troubles, and their afflictions, and shall be [clifted up](#note3c) at the last day.

4 And I would not that ye think that I [aknow](#note4a) of myself—not of the temporal but of the spiritual, not of the [bcarnal](#note4b) mind but of God.

5 Now, behold, I say unto you, if I had not been [aborn of God](#note5a) I should [bnot](#note5b) have known these things; but God has, by the mouth of his holy [cangel](#note5c), made these things known unto me, not of any [dworthiness](#note5d) of myself;

6 For I went about with the sons of Mosiah, seeking to [adestroy](#note6a) the church of God; but behold, God sent his holy angel to stop us by the way.

7 And behold, he spake unto us, as it were the voice of thunder, and the whole earth did [atremble](#note7a) beneath our feet; and we all fell to the earth, for the [bfear](#note7b) of the Lord came upon us.

8 But behold, the voice said unto me: Arise. And I arose and stood up, and beheld the angel.

9 And he said unto me: If thou wilt of thyself be destroyed, seek no more to destroy the church of God.

10 And it came to pass that I fell to the earth; and it was for the space of [athree](#note10a) days and three nights that I could not open my mouth, neither had I the use of my limbs.

11 And the angel spake more things unto me, which were heard by my brethren, but I did [anot hear](#note11a) them; for when I heard the words—If thou wilt be destroyed of thyself, seek no more to destroy the church of God—I was struck with such great fear and amazement lest perhaps I should be destroyed, that I fell to the earth and I did hear no more.

12 But I was racked with [aeternal](#note12a) [btorment](#note12b), for my soul was [charrowed up](#note12c) to the greatest degree and racked with all my sins.

13 Yea, I did remember all my sins and iniquities, for which I was [atormented](#note13a) with the [bpains](#note13b) of hell; yea, I saw that I had [crebelled](#note13c) against my God, and that I had not kept his holy commandments.

14 Yea, and I had [amurdered](#note14a) many of his children, or rather led them away unto destruction; yea, and in fine so great had been my iniquities, that the very thought of coming into the presence of my God did rack my soul with inexpressible horror.

15 Oh, thought I, that I [acould](#note15a) be banished and become extinct both soul and body, that I might not be brought to stand in the presence of my God, to be judged of my [bdeeds](#note15b).

16 And now, for three days and for three nights was I racked, even with the [apains](#note16a) of a [bdamned](#note16b) soul.

17 And it came to pass that as I was thus [aracked](#note17a) with torment, while I was [bharrowed up](#note17b) by the [cmemory](#note17c) of my many sins, behold, I [dremembered](#note17d) also to have heard my father prophesy unto the people concerning the coming of one Jesus Christ, a Son of God, to atone for the sins of the world.

18 Now, as my mind caught hold upon this thought, I cried within my heart: O Jesus, thou Son of God, [ahave](#note18a) mercy on me, who am [bin](#note18b) the [cgall of bitterness](#note18c), and am encircled about by the everlasting [dchains](#note18d) of [edeath](#note18e).

19 And now, behold, when I thought this, I could remember my [apains](#note19a) [bno more](#note19b); yea, I was harrowed up by the memory of my sins no more.

20 And oh, what [ajoy](#note20a), and what marvelous light I did behold; yea, my soul was filled with joy as exceeding as was my pain!

21 Yea, I say unto you, my son, that there could be nothing so exquisite and so bitter as were my pains. Yea, and again I say unto you, my son, that on the other hand, there can be nothing so exquisite and sweet as was my joy.

22 Yea, methought I saw, even as our father [aLehi](#note22a) saw, God sitting upon his throne, surrounded with numberless concourses of angels, in the attitude of singing and [bpraising](#note22b) their God; yea, and my soul did long to be there.

23 But behold, my limbs did receive their [astrength](#note23a) again, and I stood upon my feet, and did manifest unto the people that I had been [bborn of God](#note23b).

24 Yea, and from that time even until now, I have labored without ceasing, that I might bring souls unto [arepentance](#note24a); that I might bring them to [btaste](#note24b) of the exceeding joy of which I did taste; that they might also be [cborn of God](#note24c), and be [dfilled](#note24d) with the Holy Ghost.

25 Yea, and now behold, O my son, the Lord doth [agive](#note25a) me exceedingly great joy in the fruit of my [blabors](#note25b);

26 For because of the [aword](#note26a) which he has imparted unto me, behold, many have been born of God, and have [btasted](#note26b) as I have tasted, and have seen eye to eye as I have seen; therefore they do know of these things of which I have spoken, as I do know; and the knowledge which I have is of God.

27 And I have been supported under trials and troubles of every kind, yea, and in all manner of afflictions; yea, God has [adelivered](#note27a) me from prison, and from bonds, and from death; yea, and I do put my trust in him, and he will still [bdeliver](#note27b) me.

28 And I know that he will [araise](#note28a) me up at the last day, to dwell with him in [bglory](#note28b); yea, and I will [cpraise](#note28c) him forever, for he has [dbrought](#note28d) our fathers out of Egypt, and he has swallowed up the [eEgyptians](#note28e) in the Red Sea; and he led them by his power into the promised land; yea, and he has delivered them out of bondage and captivity from time to time.

29 Yea, and he has also brought our fathers out of the land of Jerusalem; and he has also, by his everlasting power, delivered them out of [abondage](#note29a) and captivity, from time to time even down to the present day; and I have always retained in remembrance their captivity; yea, and ye also ought to retain in remembrance, as I have done, their captivity.

30 But behold, my son, this is not all; for ye ought to know as I do know, that [ainasmuch](#note30a) as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall [bprosper](#note30b) in the land; and ye ought to know also, that inasmuch as ye will not keep the commandments of God ye shall be cut off from his presence. Now this is according to his word.

Chapter 37

The plates of brass and other scriptures are preserved to bring souls to salvation—The Jaredites were destroyed because of their wickedness—Their secret oaths and covenants must be kept from the people—Counsel with the Lord in all your doings—As the Liahona guided the Nephites, so the word of Christ leads men to eternal life. About 74 B.C.

1 And now, my son Helaman, I command you that ye take the [arecords](#note1a) which have been [bentrusted](#note1b) with me;

2 And I also command you that ye keep a [arecord](#note2a) of this people, according as I have done, upon the plates of Nephi, and keep all these things sacred which I have kept, even as I have kept them; for it is for a [bwise](#note2b) purpose that they are kept.

3 And these [aplates of brass](#note3a), which contain these engravings, which have the records of the holy scriptures upon them, which have the [bgenealogy](#note3b) of our forefathers, even from the beginning—

4 Behold, it has been prophesied by our fathers, that they should be kept and [ahanded](#note4a) down from one generation to another, and be kept and preserved by the hand of the Lord until they should go forth unto every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, that they shall know of the [bmysteries](#note4b) contained thereon.

5 And now behold, if they are kept they must retain their brightness; yea, and they will retain their brightness; yea, and also shall all the plates which do contain that which is holy writ.

6 Now ye may suppose that this is [afoolishness](#note6a) in me; but behold I say unto you, that by [bsmall](#note6b) and simple things are great things brought to pass; and small means in many instances doth confound the wise.

7 And the Lord God doth work by [ameans](#note7a) to bring about his great and eternal purposes; and by very [bsmall](#note7b) means the Lord doth [cconfound](#note7c) the wise and bringeth about the salvation of many souls.

8 And now, it has hitherto been wisdom in God that these things should be preserved; for behold, [athey](#note8a) have [benlarged](#note8b) the memory of this people, yea, and convinced many of the error of their ways, and brought them to the [cknowledge](#note8c) of their God unto the salvation of their souls.

9 Yea, I say unto you, [awere](#note9a) it not for these things that these records do contain, which are on these plates, Ammon and his brethren could not have [bconvinced](#note9b) so many thousands of the Lamanites of the incorrect tradition of their fathers; yea, these records and their [cwords](#note9c) brought them unto repentance; that is, they brought them to the knowledge of the Lord their God, and to rejoice in Jesus Christ their Redeemer.

10 And who knoweth but what they will be the [ameans](#note10a) of bringing many thousands of them, yea, and also many thousands of our [bstiffnecked](#note10b) brethren, the Nephites, who are now hardening their hearts in sin and iniquities, to the knowledge of their Redeemer?

11 Now these mysteries are not yet fully made known unto me; therefore I shall forbear.

12 And it may suffice if I only say they are preserved for a [awise](#note12a) purpose, which purpose is known unto God; for he doth [bcounsel](#note12b) in wisdom over all his works, and his paths are straight, and his course is [cone](#note12c) eternal round.

13 O remember, remember, my son Helaman, how [astrict](#note13a) are the commandments of God. And he said: [bIf](#note13b) ye will keep my commandments ye shall [cprosper](#note13c) in the land—but if ye keep not his commandments ye shall be cut off from his presence.

14 And now remember, my son, that God has [aentrusted](#note14a) you with these things, which are [bsacred](#note14b), which he has kept sacred, and also which he will keep and [cpreserve](#note14c) for a [dwise](#note14d) purpose in him, that he may show forth his power unto future generations.

15 And now behold, I tell you by the spirit of prophecy, that if ye transgress the commandments of God, behold, these things which are sacred shall be taken away from you by the power of God, and ye shall be delivered up unto Satan, that he may sift you as chaff before the wind.

16 But if ye keep the commandments of God, and do with these things which are sacred according to that which the Lord doth command you, (for you must appeal unto the Lord for all things whatsoever ye must do with them) behold, no power of earth or hell can [atake](#note16a) them from you, for God is powerful to the fulfilling of all his words.

17 For he will fulfil all his [apromises](#note17a) which he shall make unto you, for he has fulfilled his promises which he has made unto our fathers.

18 For he promised unto them that he would [apreserve](#note18a) these things for a wise purpose in him, that he might show forth his power unto future generations.

19 And now behold, one purpose hath he fulfilled, even to the restoration of [amany](#note19a) thousands of the Lamanites to the knowledge of the truth; and he hath shown forth his power in them, and he will also still show forth his power in them unto [bfuture](#note19b) generations; therefore they shall be preserved.

20 Therefore I command you, my son Helaman, that ye be diligent in fulfilling all my words, and that ye be diligent in keeping the commandments of God as they are written.

21 And now, I will speak unto you concerning those [atwenty-four](#note21a) plates, that ye keep them, that the [bmysteries](#note21b) and the works of darkness, and their secret works, or the secret works of those people who have been destroyed, may be made [cmanifest](#note21c) unto this people; yea, all their murders, and robbings, and their plunderings, and all their wickedness and abominations, may be made manifest unto this people; yea, and that ye preserve these [dinterpreters](#note21d).

22 For behold, the Lord saw that his people began to work in darkness, yea, work secret murders and abominations; therefore the Lord said, if they did not repent they should be destroyed from off the face of the earth.

23 And the Lord said: I will prepare unto my servant Gazelem, a [astone](#note23a), which shall shine forth in darkness unto light, that I may [bdiscover](#note23b) unto my people who serve me, that I may discover unto them the works of their brethren, yea, their secret works, their works of darkness, and their wickedness and abominations.

24 And now, my son, these [ainterpreters](#note24a) were prepared that the word of God might be fulfilled, which he spake, saying:

25 I will [abring forth](#note25a) out of darkness unto light all their secret works and their abominations; and except they repent I will [bdestroy](#note25b) them from off the face of the earth; and I will bring to light all their secrets and abominations, unto every nation that shall hereafter possess the land.

26 And now, my son, we see that they did not repent; therefore they have been destroyed, and thus far the word of God has been fulfilled; yea, their [asecret abominations](#note26a) have been brought out of darkness and made known unto us.

27 And now, my son, I command you that ye retain all their oaths, and their covenants, and their agreements in their secret abominations; yea, and all their [asigns](#note27a) and their wonders ye shall [bkeep](#note27b) from this people, that they know them not, lest peradventure they should fall into darkness also and be destroyed.

28 For behold, there is a [acurse](#note28a) upon all this land, that destruction shall come upon all those workers of darkness, according to the power of God, when they are fully ripe; therefore I desire that this people might not be destroyed.

29 Therefore ye shall keep these secret plans of their [aoaths](#note29a) and their covenants from this people, and only their wickedness and their murders and their abominations shall ye make known unto them; and ye shall teach them to [babhor](#note29b) such wickedness and abominations and murders; and ye shall also teach them that these people were destroyed on account of their wickedness and abominations and their murders.

30 For behold, they [amurdered](#note30a) all the prophets of the Lord who came among them to declare unto them concerning their iniquities; and the blood of those whom they murdered did cry unto the Lord their God for vengeance upon those who were their murderers; and thus the judgments of God did come upon these workers of darkness and secret [bcombinations](#note30b).

31 Yea, and [acursed](#note31a) be the land forever and ever unto those workers of darkness and secret combinations, even unto destruction, except they repent before they are fully [bripe](#note31b).

32 And now, my son, remember the words which I have spoken unto you; trust not those secret plans unto this people, but teach them an everlasting [ahatred](#note32a) against sin and iniquity.

33 [aPreach](#note33a) unto them repentance, and faith on the Lord Jesus Christ; teach them to humble themselves and to be [bmeek](#note33b) and lowly in heart; teach them to [cwithstand](#note33c) every [dtemptation](#note33d) of the devil, with their faith on the Lord Jesus Christ.

34 Teach them to never be weary of good works, but to be meek and lowly in heart; for such shall find [arest](#note34a) to their souls.

35 O, remember, my son, and [alearn](#note35a) [bwisdom](#note35b) in thy [cyouth](#note35c); yea, learn in thy youth to keep the commandments of God.

36 Yea, and [acry](#note36a) unto God for all thy support; yea, let all thy [bdoings](#note36b) be unto the Lord, and whithersoever thou goest let it be in the Lord; yea, let all thy [cthoughts](#note36c) be directed unto the Lord; yea, let the affections of thy heart be placed upon the Lord forever.

37 [aCounsel](#note37a) with the Lord in all thy doings, and he will direct thee for [bgood](#note37b); yea, when thou liest down at night lie down unto the Lord, that he may watch over you in your sleep; and when thou risest in the [cmorning](#note37c) let thy heart be full of thanks unto God; and if ye do these things, ye shall be lifted up at the last day.

38 And now, my son, I have somewhat to say concerning the thing which our fathers call a ball, or director—or our fathers called it [aLiahona](#note38a), which is, being interpreted, a compass; and the Lord prepared it.

39 And behold, there cannot any man work after the manner of so curious a workmanship. And behold, it was prepared to show unto our fathers the course which they should travel in the wilderness.

40 And it did work for them according to their [afaith](#note40a) in God; therefore, if they had faith to believe that God could cause that those spindles should point the way they should go, behold, it was done; therefore they had this miracle, and also many other miracles wrought by the power of God, day by day.

41 Nevertheless, because those miracles were worked by [asmall](#note41a) means it did show unto them marvelous works. They were [bslothful](#note41b), and forgot to exercise their faith and diligence and then those marvelous works ceased, and they did not progress in their journey;

42 Therefore, they tarried in the wilderness, or did [anot](#note42a) travel a direct course, and were afflicted with hunger and thirst, because of their transgressions.

43 And now, my son, I would that ye should understand that these things are not without a [ashadow](#note43a); for as our fathers were slothful to give heed to this compass (now these things were temporal) they did not prosper; even so it is with things which are spiritual.

44 For behold, it is as easy to give heed to the [aword](#note44a) of Christ, which will point to you a straight course to eternal bliss, as it was for our fathers to give heed to this compass, which would point unto them a straight course to the promised land.

45 And now I say, is there not a [atype](#note45a) in this thing? For just as surely as this director did bring our fathers, by following its course, to the promised land, shall the words of Christ, if we follow their course, carry us beyond this vale of sorrow into a far better land of promise.

46 O my son, do not let us be [aslothful](#note46a) because of the [beasiness](#note46b) of the [cway](#note46c); for so was it with our fathers; for so was it prepared for them, that if they would [dlook](#note46d) they might [elive](#note46e); even so it is with us. The way is prepared, and if we will look we may live forever.

47 And now, my son, see that ye take [acare](#note47a) of these sacred things, yea, see that ye [blook](#note47b) to God and live. Go unto this people and declare the word, and be sober. My son, farewell.

The commandments of Alma to his son Shiblon.

Comprising chapter 38.

Chapter 38

Shiblon was persecuted for righteousness’ sake—Salvation is in Christ, who is the life and the light of the world—Bridle all your passions. About 74 B.C.

1 My [ason](#note1a), give ear to my words, for I say unto you, even as I said unto Helaman, that [binasmuch](#note1b) as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land; and inasmuch as ye will not keep the commandments of God ye shall be [ccut off](#note1c) from his [dpresence](#note1d).

2 And now, my son, I trust that I shall have great joy in you, because of your [asteadiness](#note2a) and your faithfulness unto God; for as you have commenced in your youth to look to the Lord your God, even so I hope that you will continue in keeping his commandments; for blessed is he that [bendureth](#note2b) to the end.

3 I say unto you, my son, that I have had great joy in thee already, because of thy faithfulness and thy diligence, and thy patience and thy long-suffering among the people of the [aZoramites](#note3a).

4 For I know that thou wast in bonds; yea, and I also know that thou wast stoned for the word’s sake; and thou didst bear all these things with [apatience](#note4a) because the Lord was [bwith thee](#note4b); and now thou knowest that the Lord did deliver thee.

5 And now my son, Shiblon, I would that ye should remember, that as much as ye shall put your [atrust](#note5a) in God even so much ye shall be [bdelivered](#note5b) out of your trials, and your [ctroubles](#note5c), and your afflictions, and ye shall be lifted up at the last day.

6 Now, my son, I would not that ye should think that I know these things of myself, but it is the Spirit of God which is in me which maketh these things known unto me; for if I had not been [aborn of God](#note6a) I should not have known these things.

7 But behold, the Lord in his great mercy sent his [aangel](#note7a) to declare unto me that I must stop the work of [bdestruction](#note7b) among his people; yea, and I have seen an angel face to face, and he spake with me, and his voice was as thunder, and it shook the whole earth.

8 And it came to pass that I was [athree](#note8a) days and three nights in the most bitter [bpain](#note8b) and [canguish](#note8c) of soul; and never, until I did cry out unto the Lord Jesus Christ for mercy, did I receive a [dremission](#note8d) of my sins. But behold, I did cry unto him and I did find peace to my soul.

9 And now, my son, I have told you this that ye may learn wisdom, that ye may learn of me that there is [ano](#note9a) other way or means whereby man can be saved, only in and through Christ. Behold, he is the life and the [blight](#note9b) of the world. Behold, he is the word of truth and [crighteousness](#note9c).

10 And now, as ye have begun to teach the word even so I would that ye should continue to teach; and I would that ye would be diligent and [atemperate](#note10a) in all things.

11 See that ye are not lifted up unto pride; yea, see that ye do not [aboast](#note11a) in your own wisdom, nor of your much strength.

12 Use [aboldness](#note12a), but not overbearance; and also see that ye [bbridle](#note12b) all your passions, that ye may be filled with love; see that ye refrain from idleness.

13 Do not [apray](#note13a) as the Zoramites do, for ye have seen that they pray to be heard of men, and to be praised for their wisdom.

14 Do not say: O God, I thank thee that we are [abetter](#note14a) than our brethren; but rather say: O Lord, forgive my [bunworthiness](#note14b), and remember my brethren in mercy—yea, acknowledge your unworthiness before God at all times.

15 And may the Lord bless your soul, and receive you at the last day into his kingdom, to sit down in peace. Now go, my son, and teach the word unto this people. Be [asober](#note15a). My son, farewell.

The commandments of Alma to his son Corianton.

Comprising chapters 39 through 42.

Chapter 39

Sexual sin is an abomination—Corianton’s sins kept the Zoramites from receiving the word—Christ’s redemption is retroactive in saving the faithful who preceded it. About 74 B.C.

1 And now, my [ason](#note1a), I have somewhat more to say unto thee than what I said unto thy brother; for behold, have ye not observed the steadiness of thy brother, his faithfulness, and his diligence in keeping the commandments of God? Behold, has he not set a good [bexample](#note1b) for thee?

2 For thou didst not give so much heed unto my words as did thy brother, among the people of the [aZoramites](#note2a). Now this is what I have against thee; thou didst go on unto boasting in thy strength and thy wisdom.

3 And this is not all, my son. Thou didst do that which was grievous unto me; for thou didst forsake the ministry, and did go over into the land of Siron among the borders of the Lamanites, after the [aharlot](#note3a) Isabel.

4 Yea, she did [asteal](#note4a) away the hearts of many; but this was no excuse for thee, my son. Thou shouldst have tended to the ministry wherewith thou wast entrusted.

5 Know ye not, my son, that these things are an abomination in the sight of the Lord; yea, most [aabominable](#note5a) above all sins save it be the shedding of innocent [bblood](#note5b) or denying the Holy Ghost?

6 For behold, if ye [adeny](#note6a) the Holy Ghost when it once has had place in you, and ye know that ye deny it, behold, this is a sin which is [bunpardonable](#note6b); yea, and whosoever murdereth against the light and knowledge of God, it is not easy for him to obtain [cforgiveness](#note6c); yea, I say unto you, my son, that it is not easy for him to obtain a forgiveness.

7 And now, my son, I would to God that ye had not been [aguilty](#note7a) of so great a crime. I would not dwell upon your crimes, to harrow up your soul, if it were not for your good.

8 But behold, ye cannot [ahide](#note8a) your crimes from God; and except ye repent they will stand as a testimony against you at the last day.

9 Now my son, I would that ye should repent and forsake your sins, and go no more after the [alusts](#note9a) of your eyes, but [bcross](#note9b) yourself in all these things; for except ye do this ye can in nowise inherit the kingdom of God. Oh, remember, and take it upon you, and [ccross](#note9c) yourself in these things.

10 And I command you to take it upon you to counsel with your elder brothers in your undertakings; for behold, thou art in thy youth, and ye stand in need to be nourished by your brothers. And give heed to their counsel.

11 Suffer not yourself to be led away by any vain or foolish thing; suffer not the devil to lead away your heart again after those wicked harlots. Behold, O my son, how great [ainiquity](#note11a) ye brought upon the [bZoramites](#note11b); for when they saw your [cconduct](#note11c) they would not believe in my words.

12 And now the Spirit of the Lord doth say unto me: [aCommand](#note12a) thy children to do good, lest they [blead](#note12b) away the hearts of many people to destruction; therefore I command you, my son, in the fear of God, that ye [crefrain](#note12c) from your iniquities;

13 That ye turn to the Lord with all your mind, might, and strength; that ye lead away the hearts of no more to do wickedly; but rather return unto them, and [aacknowledge](#note13a) your faults and that wrong which ye have done.

14 [aSeek not](#note14a) after riches nor the vain things of this world; for behold, you cannot carry them with you.

15 And now, my son, I would say somewhat unto you concerning the [acoming](#note15a) of Christ. Behold, I say unto you, that it is he that surely shall come to take away the sins of the world; yea, he cometh to declare glad tidings of salvation unto his people.

16 And now, my son, this was the ministry unto which ye were called, to declare these glad tidings unto this people, to prepare their minds; or rather that salvation might come unto them, that they may prepare the minds of their [achildren](#note16a) to hear the word at the time of his coming.

17 And now I will ease your mind somewhat on this subject. Behold, you marvel why these things should be known so long beforehand. Behold, I say unto you, is not a soul at this time as precious unto God as a soul will be at the time of his coming?

18 Is it not as necessary that the plan of redemption should be [amade](#note18a) known unto this people as well as unto their children?

19 Is it not as easy at this time for the Lord to [asend](#note19a) his angel to declare these glad tidings unto us as unto our children, or as after the time of his coming?

Chapter 40

Christ brings to pass the resurrection of all men—The righteous dead go to paradise and the wicked to outer darkness to await the day of their resurrection—All things will be restored to their proper and perfect frame in the Resurrection. About 74 B.C.

1 Now my son, here is somewhat more I would say unto thee; for I perceive that thy mind is worried concerning the resurrection of the dead.

2 Behold, I say unto you, that there is no resurrection—or, I would say, in other words, that this mortal does not put on [aimmortality](#note2a), this corruption does not [bput](#note2b) on incorruption—[cuntil](#note2c) after the coming of Christ.

3 Behold, he bringeth to pass the [aresurrection](#note3a) of the dead. But behold, my son, the resurrection is not yet. Now, I unfold unto you a mystery; nevertheless, there are many [bmysteries](#note3b) which are [ckept](#note3c), that no one knoweth them save God himself. But I show unto you one thing which I have inquired diligently of God that I might know—that is concerning the resurrection.

4 Behold, there is a time appointed that all shall [acome](#note4a) forth from the dead. Now when this time cometh no one knows; but God knoweth the time which is appointed.

5 Now, whether there shall be one time, or a [asecond](#note5a) time, or a third time, that men shall come forth from the dead, it mattereth not; for God [bknoweth](#note5b) all these things; and it sufficeth me to know that this is the case—that there is a time appointed that all shall rise from the dead.

6 Now there must needs be a space betwixt the time of death and the time of the resurrection.

7 And now I would inquire what becometh of the [asouls](#note7a) of men [bfrom](#note7b) this time of death to the time appointed for the resurrection?

8 Now whether there is more than one [atime](#note8a) appointed for men to rise it mattereth not; for all do not die at once, and this mattereth not; all is as one day with God, and time only is measured unto men.

9 Therefore, there is a time appointed unto men that they shall rise from the dead; and there is a space between the time of death and the resurrection. And now, concerning this space of time, what becometh of the souls of men is the thing which I have inquired diligently of the Lord to know; and this is the thing of which I do know.

10 And when the time cometh when all shall rise, then shall they know that God [aknoweth](#note10a) all the [btimes](#note10b) which are appointed unto man.

11 Now, concerning the [astate](#note11a) of the soul between [bdeath](#note11b) and the resurrection—Behold, it has been made known unto me by an angel, that the spirits of all men, as soon as they are departed from this mortal body, yea, the spirits of all men, whether they be good or evil, are [ctaken](#note11c) [dhome](#note11d) to that God who gave them life.

12 And then shall it come to pass, that the spirits of those who are righteous are received into a state of [ahappiness](#note12a), which is called [bparadise](#note12b), a state of rest, a state of [cpeace](#note12c), where they shall rest from all their troubles and from all care, and sorrow.

13 And then shall it come to pass, that the [aspirits](#note13a) of the wicked, yea, who are evil—for behold, they have no part nor portion of the Spirit of the Lord; for behold, they chose evil works rather than good; therefore the spirit of the [bdevil](#note13b) did enter into them, and take possession of their house—and these shall be cast out into [couter darkness](#note13c); there shall be [dweeping](#note13d), and wailing, and gnashing of teeth, and this because of their own iniquity, being led captive by the will of the devil.

14 Now this is the state of the [asouls](#note14a) of the [bwicked](#note14b), yea, in darkness, and a state of awful, [cfearful](#note14c) looking for the fiery [dindignation](#note14d) of the wrath of God upon them; thus they remain in this [estate](#note14e), as well as the righteous in paradise, until the time of their resurrection.

15 Now, there are some that have understood that this state of happiness and this state of misery of the soul, before the resurrection, was a first resurrection. Yea, I admit it may be termed a resurrection, the [araising](#note15a) of the spirit or the soul and their consignation to happiness or misery, according to the words which have been spoken.

16 And behold, again it hath been spoken, that there is a [afirst](#note16a) [bresurrection](#note16b), a resurrection of all those who have been, or who are, or who shall be, down to the resurrection of Christ from the dead.

17 Now, we do not suppose that this first resurrection, which is spoken of in this manner, can be the resurrection of the souls and their [aconsignation](#note17a) to happiness or misery. Ye cannot suppose that this is what it meaneth.

18 Behold, I say unto you, Nay; but it meaneth the [areuniting](#note18a) of the soul with the body, of those from the days of Adam down to the resurrection of Christ.

19 Now, whether the souls and the bodies of those of whom has been spoken shall all be reunited at once, the wicked as well as the righteous, I do not say; let it suffice, that I say that they all come forth; or in other words, their resurrection cometh to pass [abefore](#note19a) the resurrection of those who die after the resurrection of Christ.

20 Now, my son, I do not say that their resurrection cometh at the resurrection of Christ; but behold, I give it as my opinion, that the souls and the bodies are reunited, of the righteous, at the resurrection of Christ, and his [aascension](#note20a) into heaven.

21 But whether it be at his resurrection or after, I do not say; but this much I say, that there is a [aspace](#note21a) between death and the resurrection of the body, and a state of the soul in [bhappiness](#note21b) or in [cmisery](#note21c) until the time which is appointed of God that the dead shall come forth, and be reunited, both soul and body, and be [dbrought](#note21d) to stand before God, and be judged according to their works.

22 Yea, this bringeth about the restoration of those things of which has been spoken by the mouths of the prophets.

23 The [asoul](#note23a) shall be [brestored](#note23b) to the [cbody](#note23c), and the body to the soul; yea, and every limb and joint shall be restored to its body; yea, even a [dhair](#note23d) of the head shall not be lost; but all things shall be restored to their proper and [eperfect](#note23e) frame.

24 And now, my son, this is the restoration of which has been [aspoken](#note24a) by the mouths of the prophets—

25 And then shall the [arighteous](#note25a) shine forth in the kingdom of God.

26 But behold, an awful [adeath](#note26a) cometh upon the wicked; for they die as to things pertaining to things of righteousness; for they are unclean, and [bno unclean thing](#note26b) can inherit the kingdom of God; but they are cast out, and consigned to partake of the fruits of their labors or their works, which have been evil; and they drink the dregs of a bitter [ccup](#note26c).

Chapter 41

In the Resurrection men come forth to a state of endless happiness or endless misery—Wickedness never was happiness—Carnal men are without God in the world—Every person receives again in the Restoration the characteristics and attributes acquired in mortality. About 74 B.C.

1 And now, my son, I have somewhat to say concerning the restoration of which has been spoken; for behold, some have [awrested](#note1a) the scriptures, and have gone far [bastray](#note1b) because of this thing. And I perceive that thy mind has been [cworried](#note1c) also concerning this thing. But behold, I will explain it unto thee.

2 I say unto thee, my son, that the plan of restoration is requisite with the justice of God; for it is requisite that all things should be restored to their proper order. Behold, it is requisite and just, according to the power and resurrection of Christ, that the soul of man should be restored to its body, and that every [apart](#note2a) of the body should be restored to itself.

3 And it is requisite with the [ajustice](#note3a) of God that men should be [bjudged](#note3b) according to their [cworks](#note3c); and if their works were good in this life, and the desires of their hearts were good, that they should also, at the last day, be [drestored](#note3d) unto that which is good.

4 And if their works are evil they shall be [arestored](#note4a) unto them for evil. Therefore, all things shall be [brestored](#note4b) to their proper order, every thing to its natural frame—[cmortality](#note4c) raised to [dimmortality](#note4d), [ecorruption](#note4e) to incorruption—raised to [fendless](#note4f) happiness to [ginherit](#note4g) the kingdom of God, or to endless misery to inherit the kingdom of the devil, the one on one hand, the other on the other—

5 The one raised to [ahappiness](#note5a) according to his desires of happiness, or good according to his desires of good; and the other to evil according to his desires of evil; for as he has desired to do evil all the day long even so shall he have his reward of evil when the night cometh.

6 And so it is on the other hand. If he hath repented of his sins, and desired righteousness until the end of his days, even so he shall be rewarded unto righteousness.

7 [aThese](#note7a) are they that are redeemed of the Lord; yea, these are they that are taken out, that are delivered from that endless night of darkness; and thus they stand or fall; for behold, they are their own [bjudges](#note7b), whether to do good or do evil.

8 Now, the decrees of God are [aunalterable](#note8a); therefore, the way is prepared that [bwhosoever](#note8b) will may [cwalk](#note8c) therein and be saved.

9 And now behold, my son, do not risk [aone](#note9a) more offense against your God upon those points of doctrine, which ye have hitherto risked to commit sin.

10 Do not suppose, because it has been spoken concerning restoration, that ye shall be restored from sin to happiness. Behold, I say unto you, [awickedness](#note10a) never was [bhappiness](#note10b).

11 And now, my son, all men that are in a state of [anature](#note11a), or I would say, in a [bcarnal](#note11b) state, are in the [cgall of bitterness](#note11c) and in the [dbonds](#note11d) of iniquity; they are [ewithout](#note11e) God in the world, and they have gone [fcontrary](#note11f) to the nature of God; therefore, they are in a state contrary to the nature of happiness.

12 And now behold, is the meaning of the word restoration to take a thing of a natural state and place it in an unnatural state, or to place it in a state opposite to its nature?

13 O, my son, this is not the case; but the meaning of the word restoration is to bring back again [aevil](#note13a) for evil, or carnal for carnal, or devilish for devilish—good for that which is good; righteous for that which is righteous; just for that which is just; merciful for that which is merciful.

14 Therefore, my son, see that you are merciful unto your brethren; deal [ajustly](#note14a), [bjudge](#note14b) righteously, and do [cgood](#note14c) continually; and if ye do all these things then shall ye receive your [dreward](#note14d); yea, ye shall have [emercy](#note14e) restored unto you again; ye shall have justice restored unto you again; ye shall have a righteous judgment restored unto you again; and ye shall have good rewarded unto you again.

15 For that which ye do [asend](#note15a) out shall return unto you again, and be restored; therefore, the word restoration more fully condemneth the sinner, and justifieth him not at all.

Chapter 42

Mortality is a probationary time to enable man to repent and serve God—The Fall brought temporal and spiritual death upon all mankind—Redemption comes through repentance—God Himself atones for the sins of the world—Mercy is for those who repent—All others are subject to God’s justice—Mercy comes because of the Atonement—Only the truly penitent are saved. About 74 B.C.

1 And now, my son, I perceive there is somewhat more which doth worry your mind, which ye cannot understand—which is concerning the [ajustice](#note1a) of God in the [bpunishment](#note1b) of the sinner; for ye do try to suppose that it is [cinjustice](#note1c) that the sinner should be consigned to a state of misery.

2 Now behold, my son, I will explain this thing unto thee. For behold, after the Lord God sent our first parents forth from the garden of [aEden](#note2a), to till the [bground](#note2b), from whence they were taken—yea, he drew out the man, and he placed at the east end of the garden of Eden, [ccherubim](#note2c), and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the tree of life—

3 Now, we see that the man had become as God, knowing good and evil; and lest he should put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat and live forever, the Lord God placed [acherubim](#note3a) and the flaming sword, that he should not partake of the fruit—

4 And thus we see, that there was a [atime](#note4a) granted unto man to repent, yea, a [bprobationary time](#note4b), a time to repent and serve God.

5 For behold, if Adam had put forth his hand immediately, and [apartaken](#note5a) of the [btree of life](#note5b), he would have lived forever, according to the word of God, having no space for repentance; yea, and also the word of God would have been void, and the great plan of salvation would have been frustrated.

6 But behold, it was appointed unto man to [adie](#note6a)—therefore, as they were cut off from the tree of life they should be cut off from the face of the earth—and man became [blost](#note6b) forever, yea, they became [cfallen](#note6c) man.

7 And now, ye see by this that our first parents were [acut off](#note7a) both temporally and spiritually from the [bpresence](#note7b) of the Lord; and thus we see they became subjects to follow after their own [cwill](#note7c).

8 Now behold, it was not expedient that man should be reclaimed from this [atemporal](#note8a) death, for that would destroy the great [bplan](#note8b) of happiness.

9 Therefore, as the soul could never die, and the [afall](#note9a) had brought upon all mankind a spiritual [bdeath](#note9b) as well as a temporal, that is, they were cut off from the presence of the Lord, it was expedient that mankind should be reclaimed from this spiritual death.

10 Therefore, as they had become [acarnal](#note10a), sensual, and devilish, by [bnature](#note10b), this [cprobationary state](#note10c) became a state for them to prepare; it became a preparatory state.

11 And now remember, my son, if it were not for the plan of redemption, (laying it aside) as soon as they were dead their souls were [amiserable](#note11a), being cut off from the presence of the Lord.

12 And now, there was no means to reclaim men from this fallen state, which [aman](#note12a) had brought upon himself because of his own [bdisobedience](#note12b);

13 Therefore, according to justice, the [aplan](#note13a) of [bredemption](#note13b) could not be brought about, only on conditions of repentance of men in this probationary state, yea, this preparatory state; for except it were for these conditions, mercy could not take effect except it should destroy the work of justice. Now the work of justice could not be destroyed; if so, God would [ccease](#note13c) to be God.

14 And thus we see that all mankind were [afallen](#note14a), and they were in the grasp of [bjustice](#note14b); yea, the justice of God, which consigned them forever to be cut off from his presence.

15 And now, the plan of mercy could not be brought about except an atonement should be made; therefore God himself [aatoneth](#note15a) for the sins of the world, to bring about the plan of [bmercy](#note15b), to appease the demands of [cjustice](#note15c), that God might be a [dperfect](#note15d), just God, and a [emerciful](#note15e) God also.

16 Now, repentance could not come unto men except there were a [apunishment](#note16a), which also was [beternal](#note16b) as the life of the soul should be, affixed [copposite](#note16c) to the plan of happiness, which was as [deternal](#note16d) also as the life of the soul.

17 Now, how could a man repent except he should [asin](#note17a)? How could he sin if there was no [blaw](#note17b)? How could there be a law save there was a punishment?

18 Now, there was a punishment affixed, and a just law given, which brought remorse of [aconscience](#note18a) unto man.

19 Now, if there was no law given—if a man [amurdered](#note19a) he should [bdie](#note19b)—would he be afraid he would die if he should murder?

20 And also, if there was no law given against sin men would not be afraid to sin.

21 And if there was [ano law](#note21a) given, if men sinned what could justice do, or mercy either, for they would have no claim upon the creature?

22 But there is a law given, and a [apunishment](#note22a) affixed, and a [brepentance](#note22b) granted; which repentance, mercy claimeth; otherwise, justice claimeth the creature and executeth the [claw](#note22c), and the law inflicteth the punishment; if not so, the works of justice would be destroyed, and God would cease to be God.

23 But God ceaseth not to be God, and [amercy](#note23a) claimeth the penitent, and mercy cometh because of the [batonement](#note23b); and the atonement bringeth to pass the [cresurrection](#note23c) of the dead; and the [dresurrection](#note23d) of the dead bringeth [eback](#note23e) men into the presence of God; and thus they are restored into his presence, to be [fjudged](#note23f) according to their works, according to the law and justice.

24 For behold, justice exerciseth all his demands, and also [amercy](#note24a) claimeth all which is her own; and thus, none but the truly penitent are saved.

25 What, do ye suppose that [amercy](#note25a) can rob [bjustice](#note25b)? I say unto you, Nay; not one whit. If so, God would cease to be God.

26 And thus God bringeth about his great and eternal [apurposes](#note26a), which were prepared [bfrom](#note26b) the foundation of the world. And thus cometh about the salvation and the redemption of men, and also their destruction and misery.

27 Therefore, O my son, [awhosoever](#note27a) will come may come and partake of the waters of life freely; and whosoever will not come the same is not compelled to come; but in the last day it shall be [brestored](#note27b) unto him according to his [cdeeds](#note27c).

28 If he has desired to do [aevil](#note28a), and has not repented in his days, behold, evil shall be done unto him, according to the restoration of God.

29 And now, my son, I desire that ye should let these things [atrouble](#note29a) you no more, and only let your sins trouble you, with that trouble which shall bring you down unto repentance.

30 O my son, I desire that ye should deny the [ajustice](#note30a) of God no more. Do not endeavor to excuse yourself in the least point because of your sins, by denying the justice of God; but do you let the justice of God, and his [bmercy](#note30b), and his long-suffering have full sway in your heart; and let it bring you down to the dust in [chumility](#note30c).

31 And now, O my son, ye are called of God to [apreach](#note31a) the word unto this people. And now, my son, go thy way, declare the word with truth and soberness, that thou mayest [bbring](#note31b) souls unto repentance, that the great plan of mercy may have claim upon them. And may God grant unto you even according to my words. Amen.
