* [◀](#ldsBACK_note19a)

  19a

  [3 Ne. 11:12 (12–15)](../3-ne/11.xhtml#p12).

Jesus Christ did show himself unto the people of Nephi, as the multitude were gathered together in the land Bountiful, and did minister unto them; and on this wise did he show himself unto them.

Beginning with chapter 11.

Chapter 11

The Father testifies of His Beloved Son—Christ appears and proclaims His Atonement—The people feel the wound marks in His hands and feet and side—They cry Hosanna—He sets forth the mode and manner of baptism—The spirit of contention is of the devil—Christ’s doctrine is that men should believe and be baptized and receive the Holy Ghost. About A.D. 34.

1 And now it came to pass that there were a great multitude [agathered](#note1a) together, of the people of Nephi, round about the temple which was in the land [bBountiful](#note1b); and they were marveling and wondering one with another, and were showing one to another the [cgreat](#note1c) and marvelous change which had taken place.

2 And they were also conversing about this Jesus Christ, of whom the [asign](#note2a) had been given concerning his death.

3 And it came to pass that while they were thus conversing one with another, they heard a [avoice](#note3a) as if it came out of heaven; and they cast their eyes round about, for they understood not the voice which they heard; and it was not a harsh voice, neither was it a loud voice; nevertheless, and notwithstanding it being a [bsmall](#note3b) voice it did [cpierce](#note3c) them that did hear to the center, insomuch that there was no part of their frame that it did not cause to quake; yea, it did pierce them to the very soul, and did cause their hearts to burn.

4 And it came to pass that again they heard the voice, and they [aunderstood](#note4a) it not.

5 And again the third time they did hear the voice, and did [aopen](#note5a) their ears to hear it; and their eyes were towards the sound thereof; and they did look steadfastly towards heaven, from whence the sound came.

6 And behold, the third time they did understand the voice which they heard; and it said unto them:

7 Behold my [aBeloved Son](#note7a), [bin](#note7b) whom I am well pleased, in whom I have glorified my name—hear ye him.

8 And it came to pass, as they understood they cast their eyes up again towards heaven; and behold, they [asaw](#note8a) a Man [bdescending](#note8b) out of heaven; and he was clothed in a white robe; and he came down and stood in the midst of them; and the eyes of the whole multitude were turned upon him, and they durst not open their mouths, even one to another, and wist not what it meant, for they thought it was an angel that had appeared unto them.

9 And it came to pass that he stretched forth his hand and spake unto the people, saying:

10 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, whom the prophets testified shall come into the world.

11 And behold, I am the [alight](#note11a) and the life of the world; and I have drunk out of that bitter [bcup](#note11b) which the Father hath given me, and have glorified the Father in [ctaking](#note11c) upon me the sins of the world, in the which I have suffered the [dwill](#note11d) of the Father in all things from the beginning.

12 And it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words the whole multitude [afell](#note12a) to the earth; for they remembered that it had been [bprophesied](#note12b) among them that Christ should [cshow](#note12c) himself unto them after his ascension into heaven.

13 And it came to pass that the [aLord](#note13a) spake unto them saying:

14 Arise and come forth unto me, that ye may [athrust](#note14a) your hands into my side, and also that ye may [bfeel](#note14b) the prints of the nails in my hands and in my feet, that ye may know that I am the [cGod](#note14c) of Israel, and the God of the whole [dearth](#note14d), and have been slain for the sins of the world.

15 And it came to pass that the multitude went forth, and thrust their hands into his side, and [adid](#note15a) feel the prints of the nails in his hands and in his feet; and this they did do, going forth one by one until they had all gone forth, and did see with their eyes and did feel with their hands, and did know of a surety and did bear record, that it was he, of whom it was written by the prophets, that should come.

16 And when they had all gone forth and had witnessed for themselves, they did cry out with one accord, saying:

17 Hosanna! Blessed be the name of the Most High God! And they did fall down at the feet of Jesus, and did [aworship](#note17a) him.

18 And it came to pass that he spake unto [aNephi](#note18a) (for Nephi was among the multitude) and he commanded him that he should come forth.

19 And Nephi arose and went forth, and [abowed](#note19a) himself before the Lord and did [bkiss](#note19b) his feet.

20 And the Lord commanded him that he should [aarise](#note20a). And he arose and stood before him.

21 And the Lord said unto him: I give unto you [apower](#note21a) that ye shall [bbaptize](#note21b) this people when I am again ascended into heaven.

22 And again the Lord called [aothers](#note22a), and said unto them likewise; and he gave unto them power to baptize. And he said unto them: On this wise shall ye baptize; and there shall be [bno disputations](#note22b) among you.

23 Verily I say unto you, that whoso repenteth of his sins through your [awords](#note23a), and [bdesireth](#note23b) to be baptized in my name, on this wise shall ye baptize them—Behold, ye shall go down and [cstand](#note23c) in the water, and in my name shall ye baptize them.

24 And now behold, these are the words which ye shall say, calling them by name, saying:

25 Having [aauthority](#note25a) given me of Jesus Christ, I baptize you in the name of the [bFather](#note25b), and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.

26 And then shall ye [aimmerse](#note26a) them in the water, and come forth again out of the water.

27 And after this manner shall ye [abaptize](#note27a) in my name; for behold, verily I say unto you, that the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost are [bone](#note27b); and I am in the Father, and the Father in me, and the Father and I are one.

28 And according as I have commanded you thus shall ye baptize. And there shall be no [adisputations](#note28a) among you, as there have hitherto been; neither shall there be disputations among you concerning the points of my doctrine, as there have hitherto been.

29 For verily, verily I say unto you, he that hath the spirit of [acontention](#note29a) is not of me, but is of the [bdevil](#note29b), who is the father of contention, and he stirreth up the hearts of men to contend with anger, one with another.

30 Behold, this is not my doctrine, to stir up the hearts of men with anger, one against another; but this is my doctrine, that such things [ashould](#note30a) be done away.

31 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, I will declare unto you my [adoctrine](#note31a).

32 And this is my [adoctrine](#note32a), and it is the doctrine which the Father hath given unto me; and I bear [brecord](#note32b) of the Father, and the Father beareth record of me, and the [cHoly Ghost](#note32c) beareth record of the Father and me; and I bear record that the Father commandeth all men, everywhere, to repent and believe in me.

33 And whoso believeth in me, and is [abaptized](#note33a), the same shall be [bsaved](#note33b); and they are they who shall [cinherit](#note33c) the kingdom of God.

34 And whoso believeth not in me, and is not [abaptized](#note34a), shall be damned.

35 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that this is my doctrine, and I bear record of it from the Father; and whoso [abelieveth](#note35a) in me believeth in the Father also; and unto him will the Father bear record of me, for he will visit him [bwith](#note35b) fire and with the [cHoly Ghost](#note35c).

36 And thus will the Father bear record of me, and the [aHoly Ghost](#note36a) will bear record unto him of the Father and me; for the Father, and I, and the Holy Ghost are [bone](#note36b).

37 And again I say unto you, ye must repent, and [abecome](#note37a) as a [blittle](#note37b) child, and be baptized in my name, or ye can in nowise receive these things.

38 And again I say unto you, ye must repent, and be baptized in my name, and become as a little [achild](#note38a), or ye can in nowise inherit the kingdom of God.

39 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that this is my [adoctrine](#note39a), and whoso [bbuildeth](#note39b) upon this buildeth upon my rock, and the [cgates](#note39c) of hell shall not prevail against them.

40 And whoso shall [adeclare](#note40a) more or less than this, and establish it for my doctrine, the same cometh of evil, and is not built upon my rock; but he buildeth upon a [bsandy](#note40b) foundation, and the gates of hell stand open to receive such when the floods come and the winds beat upon them.

41 Therefore, go forth unto this people, and declare the words which I have spoken, unto the ends of the earth.

Chapter 12

Jesus calls and commissions the twelve disciples—He delivers to the Nephites a discourse similar to the Sermon on the Mount—He speaks the Beatitudes—His teachings transcend and take precedence over the law of Moses—Men are commanded to be perfect even as He and His Father are perfect—Compare Matthew 5. About A.D. 34.

1 [aAnd](#note1a) it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words unto Nephi, and to those who had been called, (now the number of them who had been called, and received power and authority to [bbaptize](#note1b), was [ctwelve](#note1c)) and behold, he stretched forth his hand unto the multitude, and cried unto them, saying: [dBlessed](#note1d) are ye if ye shall give heed unto the words of these twelve whom I have [echosen](#note1e) from among you to minister unto you, and to be your servants; and unto them I have given power that they may baptize you with water; and after that ye are baptized with water, behold, I will baptize you with fire and with the Holy Ghost; therefore blessed are ye if ye shall believe in me and be baptized, after that ye have seen me and know that I am.

2 And again, more blessed are they who shall [abelieve](#note2a) in your words because that ye shall testify that ye have seen me, and that ye know that I am. Yea, blessed are they who shall [bbelieve](#note2b) in your [cwords](#note2c), and [dcome](#note2d) down into the depths of humility and be baptized, for they shall be visited [ewith](#note2e) fire and with the Holy Ghost, and shall receive a remission of their sins.

3 Yea, blessed are the [apoor in spirit](#note3a) who [bcome](#note3b) unto me, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

4 And again, blessed are all they that [amourn](#note4a), for they shall be [bcomforted](#note4b).

5 And blessed are the [ameek](#note5a), for they shall inherit the [bearth](#note5b).

6 And blessed are all they who do [ahunger](#note6a) and [bthirst](#note6b) after [crighteousness](#note6c), for they shall be [dfilled](#note6d) with the Holy Ghost.

7 And blessed are the [amerciful](#note7a), for they shall obtain mercy.

8 And blessed are all the [apure](#note8a) in heart, for they shall [bsee](#note8b) God.

9 And blessed are all the [apeacemakers](#note9a), for they shall be called the [bchildren](#note9b) of God.

10 And blessed are all they who are [apersecuted](#note10a) for my name’s sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

11 And blessed are ye when men shall [arevile](#note11a) you and persecute, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake;

12 For [aye](#note12a) shall have great joy and be exceedingly glad, for great shall be your [breward](#note12b) in heaven; for so [cpersecuted](#note12c) they the prophets who were before you.

13 Verily, verily, I say unto you, I give unto you to be the [asalt](#note13a) of the earth; but if the salt shall lose its savor wherewith shall the earth be salted? The salt shall be thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out and to be trodden under foot of men.

14 Verily, verily, I say unto you, I give unto you to be the light of this people. A city that is set on a hill cannot be hid.

15 Behold, do men light a [acandle](#note15a) and put it under a bushel? Nay, but on a candlestick, and it giveth light to all that are in the house;

16 Therefore let your [alight](#note16a) so shine before this people, that they may see your good works and [bglorify](#note16b) your Father who is in heaven.

17 Think not that I am come to destroy the law or the prophets. I am not come to destroy but to fulfil;

18 For verily I say unto you, [aone](#note18a) jot nor one tittle hath not passed away from the [blaw](#note18b), but in me it hath all been fulfilled.

19 And behold, I have given you the law and the commandments of my Father, that ye shall believe in me, and that ye shall repent of your sins, and come unto me with a [abroken heart](#note19a) and a contrite spirit. Behold, ye have the commandments before you, and the [blaw](#note19b) is fulfilled.

20 Therefore [acome](#note20a) unto me and be ye saved; for verily I say unto you, that except ye shall keep my [bcommandments](#note20b), which I have commanded you at this time, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.

21 Ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, and it is also written before you, that thou shalt not [akill](#note21a), and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment of God;

22 But I say unto you, that whosoever is [aangry](#note22a) with his brother shall be in danger of his judgment. And whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council; and whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.

23 Therefore, [aif](#note23a) ye shall come unto me, or shall desire to come unto me, and rememberest that thy brother hath aught against thee—

24 Go thy way unto thy brother, and first be [areconciled](#note24a) to thy brother, and then come unto me with full [bpurpose](#note24b) of heart, and I will receive you.

25 [aAgree](#note25a) with thine adversary quickly while thou art in the way with him, lest at any time he shall get thee, and thou shalt be cast into prison.

26 Verily, verily, I say unto thee, thou shalt by no means come out thence until thou hast paid the uttermost senine. And while ye are in prison can ye pay even one [asenine](#note26a)? Verily, verily, I say unto you, Nay.

27 Behold, it is written by them of old time, that thou shalt not commit [aadultery](#note27a);

28 But I say unto you, that whosoever looketh on a woman, to [alust after](#note28a) her, hath committed adultery already in his heart.

29 Behold, I give unto you a commandment, that ye suffer [anone](#note29a) of these things to enter into your [bheart](#note29b);

30 For it is better that ye should deny yourselves of these things, wherein ye will take up your [across](#note30a), than that ye should be cast into hell.

31 It hath been written, that whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of [adivorcement](#note31a).

32 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that whosoever shall [aput away](#note32a) his wife, saving for the cause of [bfornication](#note32b), causeth her to commit [cadultery](#note32c); and whoso shall marry her who is divorced committeth adultery.

33 And again it is written, thou shalt not [aforswear](#note33a) thyself, but shalt [bperform](#note33b) unto the Lord thine [coaths](#note33c);

34 But verily, verily, I say unto you, [aswear](#note34a) not at all; neither by heaven, for it is God’s throne;

35 Nor by the earth, for it is his footstool;

36 Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair black or white;

37 But let your [acommunication](#note37a) be [bYea](#note37b), yea; Nay, nay; for whatsoever cometh of more than these is evil.

38 And behold, it is written, an [aeye](#note38a) for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth;

39 But I say unto you, that ye shall not [aresist](#note39a) evil, but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right [bcheek](#note39b), [cturn](#note39c) to him the other also;

40 And if any man will sue thee at the law and take away thy coat, [alet](#note40a) him have thy cloak also;

41 And whosoever shall compel thee to [ago](#note41a) a mile, go with him twain.

42 [aGive](#note42a) to him that asketh thee, and from him that would [bborrow](#note42b) of thee turn thou not away.

43 And behold it is written also, that thou shalt love thy neighbor and hate thine enemy;

44 But behold I say unto you, love your [aenemies](#note44a), bless them that curse you, do [bgood](#note44b) to them that hate you, and [cpray](#note44c) for them who despitefully use you and persecute you;

45 That ye may be the children of your Father who is in heaven; for he maketh his sun to rise [aon](#note45a) the evil and on the good.

46 Therefore those things which were of old time, which were under the law, in me are all [afulfilled](#note46a).

47 [aOld](#note47a) things are done away, and all things have become [bnew](#note47b).

48 Therefore I would that ye should be [aperfect](#note48a) even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect.

Chapter 13

Jesus teaches the Nephites the Lord’s Prayer—They are to lay up treasures in heaven—The twelve disciples in their ministry are commanded to take no thought for temporal things—Compare Matthew 6. About A.D. 34.

1 [aVerily](#note1a), verily, I say that I would that ye should do alms unto the poor; but take heed that ye do not your alms before men to be seen of them; otherwise ye have no reward of your Father who is in heaven.

2 Therefore, when ye shall do your alms do not sound a trumpet before you, as will hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have [aglory](#note2a) of men. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.

3 But when thou doest alms let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth;

4 That thine alms may be in secret; and thy Father who seeth in secret, himself shall reward thee openly.

5 And when thou [aprayest](#note5a) thou shalt not do as the [bhypocrites](#note5b), for they love to pray, standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.

6 But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast [ashut](#note6a) thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father, who [bseeth](#note6b) in secret, shall reward thee openly.

7 But when ye pray, use not [avain repetitions](#note7a), as the [bheathen](#note7b), for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.

8 Be not ye therefore like unto them, for your Father [aknoweth](#note8a) what things ye have need of before ye [bask](#note8b) him.

9 After this [amanner](#note9a) therefore [bpray](#note9b) ye: Our [cFather](#note9c) who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name.

10 Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

11 And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.

12 And [alead](#note12a) us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.

13 For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, forever. Amen.

14 For, if ye [aforgive](#note14a) men their trespasses your heavenly Father will also forgive you;

15 But if ye forgive not men their trespasses neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

16 Moreover, when ye [afast](#note16a) be not as the [bhypocrites](#note16b), of a sad countenance, for they disfigure their faces that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.

17 But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thy head, and [awash](#note17a) thy face;

18 That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father, who is in [asecret](#note18a); and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.

19 Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where [amoth](#note19a) and rust doth corrupt, and thieves break through and steal;

20 But lay up for yourselves [atreasures](#note20a) in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal.

21 For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

22 The [alight](#note22a) of the body is the [beye](#note22b); if, therefore, thine eye be [csingle](#note22c), thy whole body shall be full of light.

23 But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If, therefore, the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!

24 No man can [aserve](#note24a) [btwo](#note24b) masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or else he will hold to the one and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and Mammon.

25 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he looked upon the [atwelve](#note25a) whom he had chosen, and said unto them: Remember the words which I have spoken. For behold, ye are they whom I have chosen to [bminister](#note25b) unto this people. Therefore I say unto you, [ctake](#note25c) no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than [draiment](#note25d)?

26 Behold the [afowls](#note26a) of the air, for they sow not, neither do they reap nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?

27 Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?

28 And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the [alilies](#note28a) of the field how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin;

29 And yet I say unto you, that even Solomon, in all his glory, was not arrayed like one of these.

30 Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which today is, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, even so will he clothe you, if ye are not of little faith.

31 Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?

32 For your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.

33 But [aseek](#note33a) ye first the [bkingdom](#note33b) of God and his righteousness, and all these things shall be added unto you.

34 Take therefore no thought for the morrow, for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. [aSufficient](#note34a) is the day unto the evil thereof.

Chapter 14

Jesus commands: Judge not; ask of God; beware of false prophets—He promises salvation to those who do the will of the Father—Compare Matthew 7. About A.D. 34.

1 [aAnd](#note1a) now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he turned again to the multitude, and did open his mouth unto them again, saying: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Judge not, that ye be not judged.

2 [aFor](#note2a) with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged; and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.

3 And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?

4 Or how wilt thou say to thy brother: Let me pull the mote out of thine eye—and behold, a beam is in thine own eye?

5 Thou [ahypocrite](#note5a), first cast the [bbeam](#note5b) out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast the mote out of thy brother’s eye.

6 Give not that which is [aholy](#note6a) unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.

7 [aAsk](#note7a), and it shall be given unto you; [bseek](#note7b), and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

8 For every one that asketh, receiveth; and he that seeketh, findeth; and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.

9 Or what man is there of you, who, if his son ask bread, will give him a stone?

10 Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?

11 If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father who is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?

12 Therefore, all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, [ado](#note12a) ye even so to them, for this is the law and the prophets.

13 Enter ye in at the [astrait gate](#note13a); for wide is the gate, and [bbroad](#note13b) is the way, which leadeth to destruction, and many there be who go in thereat;

14 Because strait is the [agate](#note14a), and [bnarrow](#note14b) is the way, which leadeth unto life, and [cfew](#note14c) there be that find it.

15 Beware of [afalse prophets](#note15a), who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.

16 Ye shall know them by their [afruits](#note16a). Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?

17 Even so every [agood](#note17a) tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.

18 A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.

19 Every tree that [abringeth](#note19a) not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.

20 Wherefore, by their [afruits](#note20a) ye shall know them.

21 Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall [aenter](#note21a) into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father who is in heaven.

22 Many will [asay](#note22a) to me in that day: Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name, and in thy name have cast out devils, and in thy name done many wonderful works?

23 And then will [aI](#note23a) profess unto them: I never [bknew](#note23b) you; [cdepart](#note23c) from me, ye that work iniquity.

24 Therefore, whoso heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, who built his house upon a [arock](#note24a)—

25 And the [arain](#note25a) descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it [bfell](#note25b) not, for it was founded upon a rock.

26 And every one that heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them not shall be likened unto a [afoolish](#note26a) man, who built his house upon the [bsand](#note26b)—

27 And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell, and great was the fall of it.

Chapter 15

Jesus announces that the law of Moses is fulfilled in Him—The Nephites are the other sheep of whom He spoke in Jerusalem—Because of iniquity, the Lord’s people in Jerusalem do not know of the scattered sheep of Israel. About A.D. 34.

1 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had ended these sayings he cast his eyes round about on the multitude, and said unto them: Behold, ye have heard the things which I [ataught](#note1a) before I ascended to my Father; therefore, whoso remembereth these sayings of mine and [bdoeth](#note1b) them, him will I [craise up](#note1c) at the last day.

2 And it came to pass that when Jesus had said these words he perceived that there were some among them who marveled, and wondered what he would concerning the law of Moses; for they understood not the saying that [aold](#note2a) things had passed away, and that all things had become new.

3 And he said unto them: Marvel not that I said unto you that old things had passed away, and that all things had become [anew](#note3a).

4 Behold, I say unto you that the [alaw](#note4a) is fulfilled that was given unto Moses.

5 Behold, [aI](#note5a) am he that gave the law, and I am he who covenanted with my people Israel; therefore, the law in me is fulfilled, for I have come to [bfulfil](#note5b) the law; therefore it hath an end.

6 Behold, I do [anot](#note6a) destroy the prophets, for as many as have not been fulfilled in me, verily I say unto you, shall all be fulfilled.

7 And because I said unto you that old things have passed away, I do not destroy that which hath been spoken concerning things which are to come.

8 For behold, the [acovenant](#note8a) which I have made with my people is not all fulfilled; but the law which was given unto Moses hath an end in me.

9 Behold, I am the [alaw](#note9a), and the [blight](#note9b). Look unto me, and endure to the end, and ye shall [clive](#note9c); for unto him that [dendureth](#note9d) to the end will I give eternal life.

10 Behold, I have given unto you the [acommandments](#note10a); therefore keep my commandments. And this is the law and the prophets, for they truly [btestified](#note10b) of me.

11 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words, he [asaid](#note11a) unto those twelve whom he had chosen:

12 Ye are my [adisciples](#note12a); and ye are a [blight](#note12b) unto this people, who are a remnant of the house of [cJoseph](#note12c).

13 And behold, this is the [aland](#note13a) of your inheritance; and the Father hath given it unto you.

14 And not at any time hath the Father given me commandment that I should [atell](#note14a) it unto your brethren at Jerusalem.

15 Neither at any time hath the Father given me commandment that I should tell unto them concerning the [aother tribes](#note15a) of the house of Israel, whom the Father hath led away out of the land.

16 This much did the Father [acommand](#note16a) me, that I should tell unto them:

17 That other sheep I have which are not of this fold; them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one [ashepherd](#note17a).

18 And now, because of [astiffneckedness](#note18a) and [bunbelief](#note18b) they [cunderstood](#note18c) not my word; therefore I was commanded to say no more of the [dFather](#note18d) concerning this thing unto them.

19 But, verily, I say unto you that the Father hath commanded me, and I tell it unto you, that ye were [aseparated](#note19a) from among them because of their iniquity; therefore it is because of their iniquity that they know not of you.

20 And verily, I say unto you again that the other tribes hath the Father separated from them; and it is because of their iniquity that they know not of them.

21 And verily I say unto you, that ye are they of whom I said: [aOther](#note21a) sheep I have which are not of this fold; them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one [bshepherd](#note21b).

22 And they understood me not, for they supposed it had been the [aGentiles](#note22a); for they understood not that the Gentiles should be [bconverted](#note22b) through their preaching.

23 And they understood me not that I said they shall hear my voice; and they understood me not that the [aGentiles](#note23a) should not at any time hear my voice—that I should not manifest myself unto them save it were by the [bHoly Ghost](#note23b).

24 But behold, ye have both heard [amy voice](#note24a), and seen me; and ye are my sheep, and ye are numbered among those whom the Father hath [bgiven](#note24b) me.

Chapter 16

Jesus will visit others of the lost sheep of Israel—In the latter days the gospel will go to the Gentiles and then to the house of Israel—The Lord’s people will see eye to eye when He brings again Zion. About A.D. 34.

1 And verily, verily, I say unto you that I have [aother sheep](#note1a), which are not of this land, neither of the land of Jerusalem, neither in any parts of that land round about whither I have been to minister.

2 For they of whom I speak are they who have not as yet heard my voice; neither have I at any time manifested myself unto them.

3 But I have received a [acommandment](#note3a) of the Father that I shall go unto them, and that they shall [bhear](#note3b) my voice, and shall be numbered among my sheep, that there may be one fold and one shepherd; therefore I go to show myself unto them.

4 And I command you that ye shall [awrite](#note4a) these sayings after I am gone, that if it so be that my people at Jerusalem, they who have seen me and been with me in my ministry, do not ask the Father in my name, that they may receive a knowledge of you by the Holy Ghost, and also of the other tribes whom they know not of, that these sayings which ye shall write shall be kept and shall be manifested unto the [bGentiles](#note4b), that through the fulness of the Gentiles, the remnant of their seed, who shall be scattered forth upon the face of the earth because of their [cunbelief](#note4c), may be brought in, or may be brought to a [dknowledge](#note4d) of me, their Redeemer.

5 And then will I [agather](#note5a) them in from the four quarters of the earth; and then will I fulfil the [bcovenant](#note5b) which the Father hath made unto all the people of the [chouse of Israel](#note5c).

6 And blessed are the [aGentiles](#note6a), because of their belief in me, in and of the Holy Ghost, which [bwitnesses](#note6b) unto them of me and of the Father.

7 Behold, because of their belief in me, saith the Father, and because of the unbelief of you, O house of Israel, in the [alatter day](#note7a) shall the truth come unto the [bGentiles](#note7b), that the fulness of these things shall be made known unto them.

8 But wo, saith the Father, unto the [aunbelieving](#note8a) of the Gentiles—for notwithstanding they have come forth upon the face of this land, and have [bscattered](#note8b) my people who are of the house of Israel; and my people who are of the house of Israel have been [ccast out](#note8c) from among them, and have been trodden under feet by them;

9 And because of the mercies of the Father unto the Gentiles, and also the judgments of the Father upon my people who are of the house of Israel, verily, verily, I say unto you, that after all this, and I have caused my people who are of the house of Israel to be smitten, and to be afflicted, and to be [aslain](#note9a), and to be cast out from among them, and to become [bhated](#note9b) by them, and to become a hiss and a byword among them—

10 And thus commandeth the Father that I should say unto you: At that day when the Gentiles shall [asin](#note10a) against my gospel, and shall reject the fulness of my gospel, and shall be [blifted up](#note10b) in the pride of their hearts above all nations, and above all the people of the whole earth, and shall be filled with all manner of lyings, and of deceits, and of mischiefs, and all manner of hypocrisy, and [cmurders](#note10c), and [dpriestcrafts](#note10d), and whoredoms, and of secret abominations; and if they shall do all those things, and shall [ereject](#note10e) the fulness of my gospel, behold, saith the Father, I will bring the fulness of my gospel from among them.

11 And then will I [aremember](#note11a) my covenant which I have made unto my people, O house of Israel, and I will bring my gospel unto them.

12 And I will show unto thee, O house of Israel, that the Gentiles shall not have power over you; but I will remember my covenant unto you, O house of Israel, and ye shall come unto the [aknowledge](#note12a) of the fulness of my gospel.

13 But if the Gentiles will repent and return unto me, saith the Father, behold they shall be [anumbered](#note13a) among my people, O house of Israel.

14 And I will not suffer my people, who are of the house of Israel, to go through among them, and tread them down, saith the Father.

15 But if they will not turn unto me, and hearken unto my voice, I will suffer them, yea, I will suffer my people, O house of Israel, that they shall go through among them, and shall [atread](#note15a) them down, and they shall be as salt that hath lost its savor, which is thenceforth good for nothing but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of my people, O house of Israel.

16 Verily, verily, I say unto you, thus hath the Father commanded me—that I should give unto [athis people](#note16a) this land for their inheritance.

17 And then the [awords](#note17a) of the prophet Isaiah shall be fulfilled, which say:

18 [aThy](#note18a) [bwatchmen](#note18b) shall lift up the voice; with the voice together shall they sing, for they shall see eye to eye when the Lord shall bring again Zion.

19 Break forth into joy, sing together, ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Lord hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem.

20 The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of God.

Chapter 17

Jesus directs the people to ponder His words and pray for understanding—He heals their sick—He prays for the people, using language that cannot be written—Angels minister to and fire encircles their little ones. About A.D. 34.

1 Behold, now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he looked round about again on the multitude, and he said unto them: Behold, my [atime is at hand](#note1a).

2 I [aperceive](#note2a) that ye are weak, that ye cannot [bunderstand](#note2b) all my words which I am commanded of the Father to speak unto you at this time.

3 Therefore, go ye unto your homes, and [aponder](#note3a) upon the things which I have said, and ask of the Father, in my name, that ye may understand, and [bprepare](#note3b) your minds for the [cmorrow](#note3c), and I come unto you again.

4 But now I [ago](#note4a) unto the Father, and also to [bshow](#note4b) myself unto the lost tribes of Israel, for they are not [clost](#note4c) unto the Father, for he knoweth whither he hath taken them.

5 And it came to pass that when Jesus had thus spoken, he cast his eyes round about again on the multitude, and beheld they were [ain tears](#note5a), and did look steadfastly upon him as if they would ask him to tarry a little longer with them.

6 And he said unto them: Behold, my bowels are filled with [acompassion](#note6a) towards you.

7 Have ye any that are [asick](#note7a) among you? Bring them hither. Have ye any that are lame, or blind, or halt, or maimed, or [bleprous](#note7b), or that are withered, or that are deaf, or that are afflicted in any manner? Bring them hither and I will [cheal](#note7c) them, for I have compassion upon you; my bowels are filled with mercy.

8 For I perceive that ye desire that I should show unto you what I have done unto your brethren at Jerusalem, for I see that your [afaith](#note8a) is [bsufficient](#note8b) that I should heal you.

9 And it came to pass that when he had thus spoken, all the multitude, with one accord, did go forth with their sick and their afflicted, and their lame, and with their [ablind](#note9a), and with their dumb, and with all them that were afflicted in any manner; and he did heal them every one as they were brought forth unto him.

10 And they did all, both they who had been healed and they who were whole, bow down at his feet, and did worship him; and as many as could come for the multitude did [akiss](#note10a) his feet, insomuch that they did bathe his feet with their tears.

11 And it came to pass that he commanded that their [alittle children](#note11a) should be brought.

12 So they brought their little children and set them down upon the ground round about him, and Jesus stood in the midst; and the multitude gave way till they had all been brought unto him.

13 And it came to pass that when they had all been brought, and Jesus stood in the midst, he commanded the multitude that they should [akneel](#note13a) down upon the ground.

14 And it came to pass that when they had knelt upon the ground, Jesus groaned within himself, and said: Father, I am [atroubled](#note14a) because of the wickedness of the people of the house of Israel.

15 And when he had said these words, he himself also [aknelt](#note15a) upon the earth; and behold he [bprayed](#note15b) unto the Father, and the things which he prayed cannot be written, and the multitude did bear record who heard him.

16 And after this manner do they bear record: The [aeye](#note16a) hath never seen, neither hath the ear heard, before, so great and marvelous things as we saw and heard Jesus speak unto the Father;

17 And no [atongue](#note17a) can speak, neither can there be written by any man, neither can the hearts of men conceive so great and marvelous things as we both saw and heard Jesus speak; and no one can conceive of the joy which filled our souls at the time we heard him pray for us unto the Father.

18 And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of praying unto the Father, he arose; but so great was the [ajoy](#note18a) of the multitude that they were overcome.

19 And it came to pass that Jesus spake unto them, and bade them arise.

20 And they arose from the earth, and he said unto them: Blessed are ye because of your faith. And [anow](#note20a) behold, my joy is full.

21 And when he had said these words, he [awept](#note21a), and the multitude bare record of it, and he took their little children, one by one, and [bblessed](#note21b) them, and prayed unto the Father for them.

22 And when he had done this he wept again;

23 And he spake unto the multitude, and said unto them: Behold your little ones.

24 And as they looked to behold they cast their eyes towards heaven, and they saw the heavens open, and they saw angels descending out of heaven as it were in the midst of fire; and they came down and [aencircled](#note24a) those little ones about, and they were encircled about with fire; and the angels did minister unto them.

25 And the multitude did see and [ahear](#note25a) and bear record; and they know that their record is true for they all of them did see and hear, every man for himself; and they were in number about two thousand and five hundred souls; and they did consist of men, women, and children.

Chapter 18

Jesus institutes the sacrament among the Nephites—They are commanded to pray always in His name—Those who eat His flesh and drink His blood unworthily are damned—The disciples are given power to confer the Holy Ghost. About A.D. 34.

1 And it came to pass that Jesus commanded his disciples that they should bring forth some [abread and wine](#note1a) unto him.

2 And while they were gone for bread and wine, he commanded the multitude that they should sit themselves down upon the earth.

3 And when the disciples had come with [abread and wine](#note3a), he took of the bread and brake and blessed it; and he gave unto the disciples and commanded that they should eat.

4 And when they had eaten and were filled, he commanded that they should give unto the multitude.

5 And when the multitude had eaten and were filled, he said unto the disciples: Behold there shall one be [aordained](#note5a) among you, and to him will I give power that he shall [bbreak](#note5b) [cbread](#note5c) and bless it and give it unto the people of my [dchurch](#note5d), unto all those who shall believe and be baptized in my name.

6 And this shall ye always observe to [ado](#note6a), even as I have done, even as I have broken bread and blessed it and given it unto you.

7 And this shall ye do in [aremembrance](#note7a) of my [bbody](#note7b), which I have shown unto you. And it shall be a testimony unto the Father that ye do always remember me. And if ye do always remember me ye shall have my Spirit to be with you.

8 And it came to pass that when he said these words, he commanded his disciples that they should take of the [awine](#note8a) of the cup and drink of it, and that they should also give unto the multitude that they might drink of it.

9 And it came to pass that they did so, and did drink of it and were filled; and they gave unto the multitude, and they did drink, and they were filled.

10 And when the disciples had done this, Jesus said unto them: Blessed are ye for this thing which ye have done, for this is fulfilling my commandments, and this doth witness unto the Father that ye are [awilling](#note10a) to do that which I have commanded you.

11 And this shall ye always do to those who repent and are baptized in my name; and ye shall do it in [aremembrance](#note11a) of my [bblood](#note11b), which I have shed for you, that ye may witness unto the Father that ye do always remember me. And if ye do always remember me ye shall have my Spirit to be with you.

12 And I give unto you a commandment that ye shall do these things. And if ye shall always do these things blessed are ye, for ye are built upon my [arock](#note12a).

13 But whoso among you shall do [amore](#note13a) or less than these are not built upon my rock, but are built upon a sandy foundation; and when the rain descends, and the floods come, and the winds blow, and beat upon them, they shall [bfall](#note13b), and the [cgates](#note13c) of hell are ready open to receive them.

14 Therefore blessed are ye if ye shall keep my commandments, which the Father hath commanded me that I should give unto you.

15 Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye must watch and [apray](#note15a) always, lest ye be tempted by the devil, and ye be led away captive by him.

16 And as I have prayed among you even so shall ye pray in my [achurch](#note16a), among my people who do repent and are baptized in my name. Behold I am the [blight](#note16b); I have set an [cexample](#note16c) for you.

17 And it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words unto his disciples, he turned again unto the multitude and said unto them:

18 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, ye must watch and pray always lest ye enter into temptation; for [aSatan](#note18a) desireth to have you, that he may sift you as wheat.

19 Therefore ye must always pray unto the Father in my name;

20 And [awhatsoever](#note20a) ye shall ask the Father in my name, which is right, believing that ye shall receive, behold it shall be given unto you.

21 [aPray](#note21a) in your families unto the Father, always in my name, that your wives and your children may be blessed.

22 And behold, ye shall [ameet](#note22a) together oft; and ye shall not forbid any man from coming unto you when ye shall meet together, but suffer them that they may come unto you and forbid them not;

23 But ye shall [apray](#note23a) for them, and shall not cast them out; and if it so be that they come unto you oft ye shall pray for them unto the Father, in my name.

24 Therefore, hold up your [alight](#note24a) that it may shine unto the world. Behold I am the [blight](#note24b) which ye shall hold up—that which ye have seen me do. Behold ye see that I have prayed unto the Father, and ye all have witnessed.

25 And ye see that I have commanded that [anone](#note25a) of you should go away, but rather have commanded that ye should come unto me, that ye might [bfeel](#note25b) and see; even so shall ye do unto the world; and whosoever breaketh this commandment suffereth himself to be led into temptation.

26 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words, he turned his eyes again upon the [adisciples](#note26a) whom he had chosen, and said unto them:

27 Behold verily, verily, I say unto you, I give unto you another commandment, and then I must go unto my [aFather](#note27a) that I may fulfil [bother](#note27b) commandments which he hath given me.

28 And now behold, this is the commandment which I give unto you, that ye shall not suffer any one knowingly to [apartake](#note28a) of my flesh and blood [bunworthily](#note28b), when ye shall minister it;

29 For whoso eateth and drinketh my flesh and [ablood](#note29a) [bunworthily](#note29b) eateth and drinketh damnation to his soul; therefore if ye know that a man is unworthy to eat and drink of my flesh and blood ye shall forbid him.

30 Nevertheless, ye shall not [acast](#note30a) him out from among you, but ye shall [bminister](#note30b) unto him and shall pray for him unto the Father, in my name; and if it so be that he repenteth and is baptized in my name, then shall ye receive him, and shall minister unto him of my flesh and blood.

31 But if he repent not he shall not be numbered among my people, that he may not destroy my people, for behold I [aknow](#note31a) [bmy](#note31b) sheep, and they are numbered.

32 Nevertheless, ye shall not cast him out of your [asynagogues](#note32a), or your places of worship, for unto such shall ye continue to minister; for ye know not but what they will return and repent, and come unto me with full purpose of heart, and I shall [bheal](#note32b) them; and ye shall be the means of bringing salvation unto them.

33 Therefore, keep these sayings which I have commanded you that ye come not under [acondemnation](#note33a); for wo unto him whom the Father condemneth.

34 And I give you these commandments because of the disputations which have been among you. And blessed are ye if ye have [ano disputations](#note34a) among you.

35 And now I go unto the Father, because it is expedient that I should go unto the Father [afor](#note35a) your sakes.

36 And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of these sayings, he touched with his [ahand](#note36a) the [bdisciples](#note36b) whom he had chosen, one by one, even until he had touched them all, and spake unto them as he touched them.

37 And the multitude heard not the words which he spake, therefore they did not bear record; but the disciples bare record that he gave them [apower](#note37a) to give the [bHoly Ghost](#note37b). And I will show unto you [chereafter](#note37c) that this record is true.

38 And it came to pass that when Jesus had touched them all, there came a [acloud](#note38a) and overshadowed the multitude that they could not see Jesus.

39 And while they were overshadowed he [adeparted](#note39a) from them, and ascended into heaven. And the disciples saw and did bear record that he ascended again into heaven.

Chapter 19

The twelve disciples minister unto the people and pray for the Holy Ghost—The disciples are baptized and receive the Holy Ghost and the ministering of angels—Jesus prays using words that cannot be written—He attests to the exceedingly great faith of these Nephites. About A.D. 34.

1 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had ascended into heaven, the multitude did disperse, and every man did take his wife and his children and did return to his own home.

2 And it was noised abroad among the people immediately, before it was yet dark, that the multitude had seen Jesus, and that he had ministered unto them, and that he would also show himself on the [amorrow](#note2a) unto the multitude.

3 Yea, and even all the night it was noised abroad concerning Jesus; and insomuch did they send forth unto the people that there were many, yea, an exceedingly great number, did labor exceedingly all that night, that they might be on the morrow in the place where Jesus should show himself unto the multitude.

4 And it came to pass that on the morrow, when the multitude was gathered together, behold, Nephi and his [abrother](#note4a) whom he had raised from the [bdead](#note4b), whose name was Timothy, and also his son, whose name was Jonas, and also Mathoni, and Mathonihah, his brother, and Kumen, and Kumenonhi, and Jeremiah, and Shemnon, and Jonas, and Zedekiah, and Isaiah—now these were the [cnames](#note4c) of the [ddisciples](#note4d) whom Jesus had chosen—and it came to pass that they went forth and stood in the midst of the multitude.

5 And behold, the multitude was [aso great](#note5a) that they did cause that they should be separated into twelve bodies.

6 And the twelve did teach the multitude; and behold, they did cause that the multitude should [akneel](#note6a) down upon the face of the earth, and should pray unto the Father in the name of Jesus.

7 And the disciples did pray unto the Father also in the name of Jesus. And it came to pass that they arose and ministered unto the people.

8 And when they had ministered those same words which Jesus had spoken—nothing varying from the words which Jesus had spoken—behold, they knelt again and prayed to the Father in the name of Jesus.

9 And they did pray for that which they most desired; and they desired that the [aHoly Ghost](#note9a) should be given unto them.

10 And when they had thus prayed they went down unto the water’s edge, and the multitude followed them.

11 And it came to pass that Nephi went down [ainto](#note11a) the water and was [bbaptized](#note11b).

12 And he came up out of the water and began to baptize. And he baptized all those whom Jesus had chosen.

13 And it came to pass when they were all baptized and had come [aup](#note13a) out of the water, the [bHoly Ghost](#note13b) did fall upon them, and they were filled with the Holy Ghost and with fire.

14 And behold, they were [aencircled](#note14a) about as if it were by fire; and it came down from heaven, and the multitude did witness it, and did bear record; and angels did come down out of heaven and did minister unto them.

15 And it came to pass that while the angels were ministering unto the disciples, behold, Jesus came and stood in the midst and ministered unto them.

16 And it came to pass that he spake unto the multitude, and commanded them that they should kneel down again upon the earth, and also that his disciples should kneel down upon the earth.

17 And it came to pass that when they had all knelt down upon the earth, he commanded his disciples that they should pray.

18 And behold, they began to pray; and they did pray unto Jesus, calling him their Lord and their God.

19 And it came to pass that Jesus departed out of the midst of them, and went a little way off from them and [abowed](#note19a) himself to the earth, and he said:

20 Father, I thank thee that thou hast given the Holy Ghost unto these whom I have [achosen](#note20a); and it is because of their belief in me that I have chosen them out of the world.

21 Father, I pray thee that thou wilt give the Holy Ghost unto all them that shall believe in their words.

22 Father, thou hast given them the Holy Ghost because they believe in [ame](#note22a); and thou seest that they believe in me because thou hearest them, and they pray unto me; and they pray unto me because I am with them.

23 And now Father, I [apray](#note23a) unto thee for them, and also for all those who shall believe on their words, that they may believe in me, that I may be in them [bas](#note23b) thou, Father, art in me, that we may be [cone](#note23c).

24 And it came to pass that when Jesus had thus prayed unto the Father, he came unto his disciples, and behold, they did still continue, without ceasing, to pray unto him; and they did not [amultiply](#note24a) many words, for it was given unto them what they should [bpray](#note24b), and they were filled with desire.

25 And it came to pass that Jesus blessed them as they did pray unto him; and his [acountenance](#note25a) did smile upon them, and the light of his [bcountenance](#note25b) did [cshine](#note25c) upon them, and behold they were as [dwhite](#note25d) as the countenance and also the garments of Jesus; and behold the whiteness thereof did exceed all the whiteness, yea, even there could be nothing upon earth so white as the whiteness thereof.

26 And Jesus said unto them: Pray on; nevertheless they did not cease to pray.

27 And he turned from them again, and went a little way off and bowed himself to the earth; and he prayed again unto the Father, saying:

28 Father, I thank thee that thou hast [apurified](#note28a) those whom I have chosen, because of their faith, and I pray for them, and also for them who shall believe on their words, that they may be purified in me, through faith on their words, even as they are purified in me.

29 Father, I pray not for the world, but for those whom thou hast given me [aout](#note29a) of the world, because of their faith, that they may be purified in me, that I may be in them as thou, Father, art in me, that we may be one, that I may be glorified in them.

30 And when Jesus had spoken these words he came again unto his disciples; and behold they did pray steadfastly, without ceasing, unto him; and he did smile upon them again; and behold they were [awhite](#note30a), even as Jesus.

31 And it came to pass that he went again a little way off and prayed unto the Father;

32 And tongue cannot speak the words which he prayed, neither can be [awritten](#note32a) by man the words which he prayed.

33 And the multitude did hear and do bear record; and their [ahearts](#note33a) were open and they did understand in their hearts the words which he prayed.

34 Nevertheless, so great and marvelous were the words which he prayed that they cannot be written, neither can they be [auttered](#note34a) by man.

35 And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of praying he came again to the disciples, and said unto them: [aSo](#note35a) great [bfaith](#note35b) have I never seen among all the Jews; wherefore I could not show unto them so great [cmiracles](#note35c), because of their [dunbelief](#note35d).

36 Verily I say unto you, there are none of them that have seen so great things as ye have seen; neither have they heard so great things as ye have heard.

Chapter 20

Jesus provides bread and wine miraculously and again administers the sacrament unto the people—The remnant of Jacob will come to the knowledge of the Lord their God and will inherit the Americas—Jesus is the prophet like unto Moses, and the Nephites are children of the prophets—Others of the Lord’s people will be gathered to Jerusalem. About A.D. 34.

1 And it came to pass that he commanded the multitude that they should [acease](#note1a) to [bpray](#note1b), and also his disciples. And he commanded them that they should not cease to pray in their hearts.

2 And he commanded them that they should arise and stand up upon their feet. And they arose up and stood upon their feet.

3 And it came to pass that he [abrake](#note3a) [bbread](#note3b) again and blessed it, and gave to the disciples to eat.

4 And when they had eaten he commanded them that they should break bread, and give unto the multitude.

5 And when they had given unto the multitude he also gave them wine to drink, and commanded them that they should give unto the multitude.

6 Now, there had been no [abread](#note6a), neither wine, brought by the disciples, neither by the multitude;

7 But he truly [agave](#note7a) unto them bread to eat, and also wine to drink.

8 And he said unto them: He that eateth this bread eateth of [amy body](#note8a) to his soul; and he that drinketh of this wine drinketh of my blood to his soul; and his soul shall never hunger nor thirst, but shall be filled.

9 Now, when the multitude had all eaten and drunk, behold, they were filled with the Spirit; and they did cry out with one voice, and gave glory to Jesus, whom they both saw and heard.

10 And it came to pass that when they had all given glory unto Jesus, he said unto them: Behold now I finish the commandment which the Father hath commanded me concerning this people, who are a remnant of the house of Israel.

11 Ye remember that I spake unto you, and said that when the [awords](#note11a) of [bIsaiah](#note11b) should be fulfilled—behold they are written, ye have them before you, therefore search them—

12 And verily, verily, I say unto you, that when they shall be fulfilled then is the fulfilling of the [acovenant](#note12a) which the Father hath made unto his people, O house of Israel.

13 And then shall the [aremnants](#note13a), which shall be [bscattered](#note13b) abroad upon the face of the earth, be [cgathered](#note13c) in from the east and from the west, and from the south and from the north; and they shall be brought to the [dknowledge](#note13d) of the Lord their God, who hath redeemed them.

14 And the Father hath [acommanded](#note14a) me that I should give unto you this [bland](#note14b), for your inheritance.

15 And I say unto you, that if the Gentiles do not [arepent](#note15a) after the [bblessing](#note15b) which they shall receive, after they have scattered my people—

16 Then shall ye, who are a [aremnant](#note16a) of the house of Jacob, go forth among them; and ye shall be in the midst of them who shall be many; and ye shall be among them as a lion among the beasts of the forest, and as a young [blion](#note16b) among the flocks of sheep, who, if he goeth through both [ctreadeth](#note16c) down and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.

17 Thy hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.

18 And I will [agather](#note18a) my people together as a man gathereth his sheaves into the floor.

19 For I will make my [apeople](#note19a) with whom the Father hath covenanted, yea, I will make thy [bhorn](#note19b) iron, and I will make thy hoofs brass. And thou shalt [cbeat](#note19c) in pieces many people; and I will consecrate their gain unto the Lord, and their substance unto the Lord of the whole earth. And behold, I am he who doeth it.

20 And it shall come to pass, saith the Father, that the [asword](#note20a) of my justice shall hang over them at that day; and except they repent it shall fall upon them, saith the Father, yea, even upon all the nations of the Gentiles.

21 And it shall come to pass that I will establish my [apeople](#note21a), O house of Israel.

22 And behold, this [apeople](#note22a) will I establish in this land, unto the fulfilling of the [bcovenant](#note22b) which I made with your father Jacob; and it shall be a [cNew Jerusalem](#note22c). And the [dpowers](#note22d) of heaven shall be in the midst of this people; yea, even [eI](#note22e) will be in the midst of you.

23 Behold, I am he of whom Moses spake, saying: [aA prophet](#note23a) shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you. And it shall come to pass that every soul who will not hear that prophet shall be cut off from among the people.

24 Verily I say unto you, yea, and [aall](#note24a) the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have testified of me.

25 And behold, ye are the [achildren](#note25a) of the prophets; and ye are of the house of Israel; and ye are of the [bcovenant](#note25b) which the Father made with your fathers, saying unto Abraham: And [cin](#note25c) thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.

26 The Father having raised me up unto you first, and sent me to [abless](#note26a) you in [bturning](#note26b) away every one of you from his iniquities; and this because ye are the children of the covenant—

27 And after that ye were blessed then fulfilleth the Father the covenant which he made with Abraham, saying: [aIn](#note27a) thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed—unto the pouring out of the Holy Ghost through me upon the Gentiles, which [bblessing](#note27b) upon the [cGentiles](#note27c) shall make them mighty above all, unto the [dscattering](#note27d) of my people, O house of Israel.

28 And they shall be a [ascourge](#note28a) unto the people of this land. Nevertheless, when they shall have received the fulness of my gospel, then if they shall harden their hearts against me I will return their [biniquities](#note28b) upon their own heads, saith the Father.

29 And I will [aremember](#note29a) the covenant which I have made with my people; and I have covenanted with them that I would [bgather](#note29b) them together in mine own due time, that I would give unto them again the [cland](#note29c) of their fathers for their inheritance, which is the land of Jerusalem, which is the promised land unto them forever, saith the Father.

30 And it shall come to pass that the time cometh, when the fulness of my gospel shall be preached unto them;

31 And they shall [abelieve](#note31a) in me, that I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and shall pray unto the Father in my name.

32 Then shall their [awatchmen](#note32a) lift up their voice, and with the voice together shall they sing; for they shall see eye to eye.

33 Then will the Father gather them together again, and give unto them [aJerusalem](#note33a) for the [bland](#note33b) of their inheritance.

34 Then shall they break forth into joy—[aSing](#note34a) together, ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Father hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem.

35 The Father hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of the Father; and the Father and I are one.

36 And then shall be brought to pass that which is written: [aAwake](#note36a), awake again, and put on thy strength, O Zion; put on thy beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city, for henceforth there shall no more come into thee the uncircumcised and the unclean.

37 Shake thyself from the dust; arise, sit down, O Jerusalem; loose thyself from the bands of thy neck, O captive daughter of Zion.

38 For thus saith the Lord: Ye have sold yourselves for naught, and ye shall be redeemed without money.

39 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that my people shall know my name; yea, in that day they shall know that I am he that doth speak.

40 And then shall they say: [aHow](#note40a) beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings unto them, that [bpublisheth](#note40b) peace; that bringeth good tidings unto them of good, that publisheth salvation; that saith unto Zion: Thy God reigneth!

41 And then shall a cry go forth: [aDepart](#note41a) ye, depart ye, go ye out from thence, touch not that which is [bunclean](#note41b); go ye out of the midst of her; be ye [cclean](#note41c) that bear the vessels of the Lord.

42 For ye shall [anot go](#note42a) out with [bhaste](#note42b) nor go by flight; for the Lord will go before you, and the God of Israel shall be your rearward.

43 Behold, my servant shall deal prudently; he shall be exalted and extolled and be very high.

44 As many were astonished at thee—his visage was so marred, more than any man, and his form more than the sons of men—

45 So shall he [asprinkle](#note45a) many nations; the kings shall shut their mouths at him, for that which had not been told them shall they see; and that which they had not heard shall they [bconsider](#note45b).

46 Verily, verily, I say unto you, all these things shall surely come, even as the Father hath commanded me. Then shall this covenant which the Father hath covenanted with his people be fulfilled; and then shall [aJerusalem](#note46a) be inhabited again with my people, and it shall be the land of their inheritance.

Chapter 21

Israel will be gathered when the Book of Mormon comes forth—The Gentiles will be established as a free people in America—They will be saved if they believe and obey; otherwise, they will be cut off and destroyed—Israel will build the New Jerusalem, and the lost tribes will return. About A.D. 34.

1 And verily I say unto you, I give unto you a [asign](#note1a), that ye may know the [btime](#note1b) when these things shall be about to take place—that I shall gather in, from their long dispersion, my people, O house of Israel, and shall establish again among them my Zion;

2 And behold, this is the thing which I will give unto you for a sign—for verily I say unto you that [awhen](#note2a) these things which I declare unto you, and which I shall declare unto you hereafter of myself, and by the power of the Holy Ghost which shall be given unto you of the Father, shall be made known unto the Gentiles that they may know concerning this people who are a remnant of the house of Jacob, and concerning this my people who shall be scattered by them;

3 Verily, verily, I say unto you, when these things shall be made [aknown](#note3a) unto them of the Father, and shall come forth of the Father, [bfrom them](#note3b) unto you;

4 For it is wisdom in the Father that they should be established in this land, and be set up as a [afree](#note4a) people by the power of the Father, that these things might come forth from them unto a remnant of your seed, that the [bcovenant](#note4b) of the Father may be fulfilled which he hath covenanted with his people, O house of Israel;

5 Therefore, when these works and the works which shall be wrought among you hereafter shall come forth [afrom](#note5a) the Gentiles, unto your [bseed](#note5b) which shall dwindle in unbelief because of iniquity;

6 For thus it behooveth the Father that it should come forth from the [aGentiles](#note6a), that he may show forth his power unto the Gentiles, for this cause that the Gentiles, if they will not harden their hearts, that they may repent and come unto me and be baptized in my name and know of the true points of my doctrine, that they may be [bnumbered](#note6b) among my people, O house of Israel;

7 And when these things come to pass that thy [aseed](#note7a) shall begin to know these things—it shall be a sign unto them, that they may know that the work of the Father hath already commenced unto the fulfilling of the covenant which he hath made unto the people who are of the house of Israel.

8 And when that day shall come, it shall come to pass that kings shall shut their mouths; for that which had not been told them shall they see; and that which they had not heard shall they [aconsider](#note8a).

9 For in that day, for my sake shall the Father [awork](#note9a) a work, which shall be a great and a [bmarvelous](#note9b) [cwork](#note9c) among them; and there shall be among them those who will not believe it, although a man shall declare it unto them.

10 But behold, the life of my servant shall be in my hand; therefore they shall not hurt him, although he shall be [amarred](#note10a) because of them. Yet I will heal him, for I will show unto them that [bmy](#note10b) wisdom is greater than the cunning of the devil.

11 Therefore it shall come to pass that whosoever will not believe in my words, who am Jesus Christ, which the Father shall cause [ahim](#note11a) to bring forth unto the [bGentiles](#note11b), and shall give unto him power that he shall bring them forth unto the Gentiles, (it shall be done even as Moses said) they shall be [ccut off](#note11c) from among my people who are of the covenant.

12 And my people who are a remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles, yea, in the midst of them as a [alion](#note12a) among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among the flocks of sheep, who, if he go through both treadeth down and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.

13 Their hand shall be lifted up upon their [aadversaries](#note13a), and all their enemies shall be cut off.

14 Yea, wo be unto the Gentiles except they [arepent](#note14a); for it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Father, that I will cut off thy horses out of the midst of thee, and I will destroy thy [bchariots](#note14b);

15 And I will cut off the cities of thy land, and throw down all thy [astrongholds](#note15a);

16 And I will cut off [awitchcrafts](#note16a) out of thy land, and thou shalt have no more soothsayers;

17 Thy [agraven images](#note17a) I will also cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee, and thou shalt no more worship the works of thy hands;

18 And I will pluck up thy [agroves](#note18a) out of the midst of thee; so will I destroy thy cities.

19 And it shall come to pass that all [alyings](#note19a), and deceivings, and envyings, and strifes, and priestcrafts, and whoredoms, shall be done away.

20 For it shall come to pass, saith the Father, that at that [aday](#note20a) whosoever will not repent and come unto my Beloved Son, them will I [bcut off](#note20b) from among my people, O house of Israel;

21 And I will execute [avengeance](#note21a) and [bfury](#note21b) upon them, even as upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.

22 But if they will repent and hearken unto my words, and [aharden](#note22a) not their hearts, I will [bestablish](#note22b) my church among them, and they shall come in unto the covenant and be [cnumbered](#note22c) among this the remnant of Jacob, unto whom I have given this land for their [dinheritance](#note22d);

23 And they shall assist my [apeople](#note23a), the remnant of Jacob, and also as many of the house of Israel as shall come, that they may build a city, which shall be called the [bNew Jerusalem](#note23b).

24 And then shall [athey](#note24a) assist my people that they may be gathered in, who are scattered upon all the face of the land, in unto the New Jerusalem.

25 And then shall the [apower](#note25a) of heaven come down among them; and [bI](#note25b) also will be in the midst.

26 And then shall the work of the Father commence at that day, even [awhen](#note26a) this gospel shall be preached among the remnant of [bthis people](#note26b). Verily I say unto you, at that day shall the work of the Father commence among all the dispersed of my people, yea, even the tribes which have been [clost](#note26c), which the Father hath led away out of Jerusalem.

27 Yea, the work shall commence among all the [adispersed](#note27a) of my people, with the Father to prepare the way whereby they may [bcome](#note27b) unto me, that they may call on the Father in my name.

28 Yea, and then shall the work commence, with the Father among all nations in preparing the way whereby his people may be [agathered](#note28a) home to the land of their inheritance.

29 And they shall go out from all nations; and they shall [anot go](#note29a) out in [bhaste](#note29b), nor go by flight, for I will go before them, saith the Father, and I will be their rearward.

Chapter 22

In the last days, Zion and her stakes will be established, and Israel will be gathered in mercy and tenderness—They will triumph—Compare Isaiah 54. About A.D. 34.

1 And then shall that which is written come to pass: Sing, O [abarren](#note1a), thou that didst not bear; break forth into [bsinging](#note1b), and cry aloud, thou that didst not travail with child; for more are the children of the [cdesolate](#note1c) than the children of the married wife, saith the Lord.

2 Enlarge the place of thy tent, and let them stretch forth the curtains of thy habitations; spare not, lengthen thy cords and strengthen thy [astakes](#note2a);

3 For thou shalt break forth on the right hand and on the left, and thy seed shall [ainherit](#note3a) the [bGentiles](#note3b) and make the desolate cities to be inhabited.

4 Fear not, for thou shalt not be ashamed; neither be thou confounded, for thou shalt not be put to [ashame](#note4a); for thou shalt forget the [bshame](#note4b) of thy youth, and shalt not remember the [creproach](#note4c) of thy youth, and shalt not remember the reproach of thy widowhood any more.

5 For thy maker, thy [ahusband](#note5a), the Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel—the God of the whole earth shall he be called.

6 For the Lord hath called thee [aas](#note6a) a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, and a wife of youth, when thou wast refused, saith thy God.

7 For a small moment have I [aforsaken](#note7a) thee, but with great mercies will I gather thee.

8 In a little wrath I hid my face from thee for a moment, but with everlasting [akindness](#note8a) will I have [bmercy](#note8b) on thee, saith the Lord thy Redeemer.

9 For [athis](#note9a), the [bwaters of Noah](#note9b) unto me, for as I have sworn that the waters of Noah should no more go over the earth, so have I sworn that I would not be wroth with thee.

10 For the [amountains](#note10a) shall depart and the hills be removed, but my [bkindness](#note10b) shall not [cdepart](#note10c) from thee, neither shall the covenant of my peace be removed, saith the Lord that hath mercy on thee.

11 O thou afflicted, tossed with tempest, and not comforted! Behold, I will lay thy [astones](#note11a) with fair colors, and lay thy foundations with sapphires.

12 And I will make thy windows of agates, and thy gates of carbuncles, and all thy borders of pleasant stones.

13 And [aall](#note13a) thy children shall be taught of the Lord; and great shall be the [bpeace](#note13b) of thy children.

14 In [arighteousness](#note14a) shalt thou be established; thou shalt be far from oppression for thou shalt not fear, and from terror for it shall not come near thee.

15 Behold, they shall surely gather together [aagainst](#note15a) thee, not by me; whosoever shall gather together against thee shall fall for thy sake.

16 Behold, I have created the smith that bloweth the coals in the fire, and that bringeth forth an instrument for his work; and I have created the waster to destroy.

17 No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper; and every tongue that shall revile against thee in judgment thou shalt condemn. This is the heritage of the [aservants](#note17a) of the Lord, and their righteousness is of me, saith the Lord.

Chapter 23

Jesus approves the words of Isaiah—He commands the people to search the prophets—The words of Samuel the Lamanite concerning the Resurrection are added to their records. About A.D. 34.

1 And now, behold, I say unto you, that ye ought to [asearch](#note1a) these things. Yea, a commandment I give unto you that ye search these things diligently; for great are the words of [bIsaiah](#note1b).

2 For surely he spake as touching all things concerning my people which are of the house of Israel; [atherefore](#note2a) it must needs be that he must speak also to the Gentiles.

3 And all things that he spake have been and [ashall](#note3a) be, even according to the words which he spake.

4 Therefore give heed to my words; write the things which I have told you; and according to the time and the will of the Father [athey](#note4a) shall go forth unto the Gentiles.

5 And whosoever will hearken unto my words and repenteth and is baptized, the same shall be saved. Search the [aprophets](#note5a), for many there be that testify of these things.

6 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had said these words he said unto them again, after he had expounded all the scriptures unto them which they had received, he said unto them: Behold, other scriptures I would that ye should write, that ye have not.

7 And it came to pass that he said unto [aNephi](#note7a): Bring forth the record which ye have kept.

8 And when Nephi had brought forth the records, and laid them before him, he cast his eyes upon them and said:

9 Verily I say unto you, I commanded my servant [aSamuel](#note9a), the Lamanite, that he should testify unto this people, that at the day that the Father should glorify his name in me that there were [bmany](#note9b) [csaints](#note9c) who should [darise](#note9d) from the dead, and should appear unto many, and should minister unto them. And he said unto them: Was it not so?

10 And his disciples answered him and said: Yea, Lord, Samuel did prophesy according to thy words, and they were all fulfilled.

11 And Jesus said unto them: How be it that ye have not [awritten](#note11a) this thing, that many [bsaints](#note11b) did arise and appear unto many and did minister unto them?

12 And it came to pass that Nephi remembered that this thing had not been written.

13 And it came to pass that Jesus commanded that it should be [awritten](#note13a); therefore it was written according as he commanded.

14 And now it came to pass that when Jesus had [aexpounded](#note14a) all the scriptures in one, which they had written, he commanded them that they should [bteach](#note14b) the things which he had expounded unto them.
